<!DOCTYPE html>

<html lang="en" data-theme="light">

<head>

<meta charset="UTF-8">

<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

<title>English Grammar in Use — Interactive Learning Platform</title>

<style>

/* ============================================================

   DESIGN TOKENS

   ============================================================ */

:root {

  --c-bg:          #F7F6F2;

  --c-surface:     #FFFFFF;

  --c-surface2:    #F0EEE8;

  --c-border:      #E2DED4;

  --c-text:        #1A1814;

  --c-text2:       #5C5850;

  --c-text3:       #9C9890;

  --c-accent:      #2B6CB0;

  --c-accent-light:#EBF4FF;

  --c-accent2:     #276749;

  --c-accent2-light:#F0FFF4;

  --c-warn:        #C05621;

  --c-warn-light:  #FFFAF0;

  --c-error:       #C53030;

  --c-error-light: #FFF5F5;

  --c-success:     #276749;

  --c-success-light:#F0FFF4;

  --c-gold:        #B7791F;

  --c-gold-light:  #FFFFF0;

  --r-sm: 6px;

  --r-md: 10px;

  --r-lg: 16px;

  --r-xl: 24px;

  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,.07), 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.05);

  --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.08), 0 2px 4px rgba(0,0,0,.05);

  --shadow-lg: 0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.10), 0 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.06);

  --font-display: 'Georgia', 'Times New Roman', serif;

  --font-body:    system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;

  --font-mono:    'Fira Code', 'Cascadia Code', monospace;

  --sidebar-w: 300px;

  --header-h:  60px;

  --trans: .2s ease;

}

[data-theme="dark"] {

  --c-bg:          #0F0E0C;

  --c-surface:     #1C1A16;

  --c-surface2:    #252318;

  --c-border:      #332F26;

  --c-text:        #F0EDE6;

  --c-text2:       #A8A398;

  --c-text3:       #6B6760;

  --c-accent:      #63B3ED;

  --c-accent-light:#1A2A3E;

  --c-accent2:     #68D391;

  --c-accent2-light:#1A2E22;

  --c-warn:        #F6AD55;

  --c-warn-light:  #2D1E0A;

  --c-error:       #FC8181;

  --c-error-light: #2D1010;

  --c-success:     #68D391;

  --c-success-light:#1A2E22;

  --c-gold:        #ECC94B;

  --c-gold-light:  #2D2510;

  --shadow-sm: 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,.3);

  --shadow-md: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,.4);

  --shadow-lg: 0 10px 30px rgba(0,0,0,.5);

}

/* ============================================================

   RESET & BASE

   ============================================================ */

*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }

html { scroll-behavior: smooth; font-size: 16px; }

body {

  font-family: var(--font-body);

  background: var(--c-bg);

  color: var(--c-text);

  line-height: 1.6;

  transition: background var(--trans), color var(--trans);

  overflow-x: hidden;

}

a { color: var(--c-accent); text-decoration: none; }

a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

button { cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit; border: none; background: none; }

input, select, textarea { font-family: inherit; }

ul, ol { list-style: none; }

img { max-width: 100%; }

/* ============================================================

   SCROLLBAR

   ============================================================ */

::-webkit-scrollbar { width: 6px; height: 6px; }

::-webkit-scrollbar-track { background: transparent; }

::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb { background: var(--c-border); border-radius: 99px; }

::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb:hover { background: var(--c-text3); }

/* ============================================================

   LAYOUT SHELL

   ============================================================ */

#app { display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 100vh; }

/* HEADER */

#header {

  position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; right: 0; z-index: 100;

  height: var(--header-h);

  background: var(--c-surface);

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;

  padding: 0 16px;

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm);

  transition: background var(--trans), border-color var(--trans);

}

#header .logo {

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;

  font-family: var(--font-display);

  font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 700;

  color: var(--c-text); white-space: nowrap;

  text-decoration: none;

}

#header .logo span { color: var(--c-accent); }

#header .logo .badge {

  font-family: var(--font-body);

  font-size: .65rem; font-weight: 600;

  background: var(--c-accent); color: #fff;

  padding: 2px 6px; border-radius: 99px;

  letter-spacing: .04em; text-transform: uppercase;

}

#search-wrap {

  flex: 1; max-width: 480px;

  position: relative;

}

#search-input {

  width: 100%; height: 36px;

  background: var(--c-surface2); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: 99px; padding: 0 36px 0 14px;

  font-size: .875rem; color: var(--c-text);

  outline: none; transition: border-color var(--trans), box-shadow var(--trans);

}

#search-input::placeholder { color: var(--c-text3); }

#search-input:focus {

  border-color: var(--c-accent);

  box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--c-accent-light);

}

#search-icon {

  position: absolute; right: 12px; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%);

  color: var(--c-text3); pointer-events: none; font-size: .85rem;

}

#search-results {

  display: none; position: absolute; top: calc(100% + 6px); left: 0; right: 0;

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-lg); box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg);

  max-height: 400px; overflow-y: auto; z-index: 200;

}

#search-results.open { display: block; }

.search-item {

  padding: 10px 14px; cursor: pointer;

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  transition: background var(--trans);

  display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px;

}

.search-item:last-child { border-bottom: none; }

.search-item:hover { background: var(--c-surface2); }

.search-item .si-num {

  font-size: .7rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--c-accent);

  background: var(--c-accent-light); padding: 1px 6px;

  border-radius: 4px; white-space: nowrap; margin-top: 2px;

}

.search-item .si-title { font-size: .875rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c-text); }

.search-item .si-cat { font-size: .75rem; color: var(--c-text3); margin-top: 1px; }

#header-actions { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; margin-left: auto; }

.hdr-btn {

  width: 36px; height: 36px; border-radius: 50%;

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;

  color: var(--c-text2); font-size: 1rem;

  background: transparent; border: none;

  transition: background var(--trans), color var(--trans);

  position: relative;

}

.hdr-btn:hover { background: var(--c-surface2); color: var(--c-text); }

.hdr-btn .badge-count {

  position: absolute; top: 2px; right: 2px;

  width: 16px; height: 16px; border-radius: 50%;

  background: var(--c-error); color: #fff;

  font-size: .6rem; font-weight: 700;

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;

}

#sidebar-toggle { display: none; }

/* BODY LAYOUT */

#body {

  display: flex; margin-top: var(--header-h);

  min-height: calc(100vh - var(--header-h));

}

/* SIDEBAR */

#sidebar {

  width: var(--sidebar-w); flex-shrink: 0;

  position: fixed; top: var(--header-h); bottom: 0; left: 0;

  background: var(--c-surface);

  border-right: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  overflow-y: auto; z-index: 90;

  transition: transform var(--trans), background var(--trans);

  display: flex; flex-direction: column;

}

#sidebar-inner { flex: 1; padding: 0 0 80px; }

.sb-section { margin-bottom: 4px; }

.sb-section-hdr {

  padding: 10px 16px 6px;

  font-size: .65rem; font-weight: 700;

  letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase;

  color: var(--c-text3);

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;

}

.sb-section-hdr .toggle-btn {

  color: var(--c-text3); font-size: .75rem;

  transition: transform var(--trans);

}

.sb-section-hdr.collapsed .toggle-btn { transform: rotate(-90deg); }

.sb-unit-list { overflow: hidden; transition: max-height .3s ease; }

.sb-unit-list.collapsed { max-height: 0 !important; }

.sb-unit {

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;

  padding: 7px 16px 7px 20px;

  cursor: pointer; font-size: .825rem;

  color: var(--c-text2);

  transition: background var(--trans), color var(--trans);

  border-left: 3px solid transparent;

  line-height: 1.3;

}

.sb-unit:hover { background: var(--c-surface2); color: var(--c-text); }

.sb-unit.active {

  background: var(--c-accent-light); color: var(--c-accent);

  border-left-color: var(--c-accent); font-weight: 600;

}

.sb-unit.completed .sb-check { color: var(--c-success); }

.sb-unit .sb-num {

  font-size: .7rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--c-text3);

  min-width: 22px; text-align: right;

}

.sb-unit.active .sb-num { color: var(--c-accent); }

.sb-unit .sb-check { margin-left: auto; font-size: .7rem; color: var(--c-border); }

/* PROGRESS BAR IN SIDEBAR */

#sb-progress-wrap {

  padding: 12px 16px;

  border-top: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  background: var(--c-surface);

}

.sb-prog-label {

  display: flex; justify-content: space-between;

  font-size: .75rem; color: var(--c-text2); margin-bottom: 6px;

}

.prog-bar-bg {

  height: 6px; background: var(--c-border); border-radius: 99px; overflow: hidden;

}

.prog-bar-fill {

  height: 100%; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--c-accent), var(--c-accent2));

  border-radius: 99px; transition: width .5s ease;

}

/* MAIN CONTENT */

#main {

  margin-left: var(--sidebar-w); flex: 1;

  max-width: calc(100vw - var(--sidebar-w));

  min-height: calc(100vh - var(--header-h));

}

/* READING PROGRESS */

#reading-progress {

  position: fixed; top: var(--header-h); left: var(--sidebar-w); right: 0; height: 3px;

  z-index: 99; background: transparent;

}

#reading-progress-bar {

  height: 100%;

  background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--c-accent), var(--c-accent2));

  width: 0%; transition: width .1s linear;

}

/* ============================================================

   HOME / DASHBOARD VIEW

   ============================================================ */

#view-home { padding: 40px 48px; max-width: 1100px; }

.home-hero {

  margin-bottom: 40px;

  padding: 40px;

  background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--c-accent-light) 0%, var(--c-accent2-light) 100%);

  border-radius: var(--r-xl);

  border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  position: relative; overflow: hidden;

}

.home-hero::before {

  content: '⟨⟩';

  position: absolute; right: 40px; top: 50%; transform: translateY(-50%);

  font-size: 8rem; color: var(--c-accent); opacity: .06;

  font-family: var(--font-display); letter-spacing: -10px;

}

.home-hero h1 {

  font-family: var(--font-display);

  font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 700;

  color: var(--c-text); margin-bottom: 8px;

  line-height: 1.25;

}

.home-hero h1 em { color: var(--c-accent); font-style: normal; }

.home-hero p {

  color: var(--c-text2); font-size: 1rem;

  max-width: 540px; margin-bottom: 24px;

}

.hero-actions { display: flex; gap: 12px; flex-wrap: wrap; }

.btn {

  display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px;

  padding: 10px 20px; border-radius: var(--r-md);

  font-size: .875rem; font-weight: 600;

  transition: all var(--trans); cursor: pointer; border: none;

}

.btn-primary {

  background: var(--c-accent); color: #fff;

}

.btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); }

.btn-outline {

  background: transparent; color: var(--c-accent);

  border: 1.5px solid var(--c-accent);

}

.btn-outline:hover { background: var(--c-accent-light); }

.btn-ghost {

  background: transparent; color: var(--c-text2);

  border: 1.5px solid var(--c-border);

}

.btn-ghost:hover { background: var(--c-surface2); }

.btn-sm { padding: 6px 14px; font-size: .8rem; }

.btn-danger { background: var(--c-error); color: #fff; }

.btn-danger:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); }

.btn-success { background: var(--c-success); color: #fff; }

/* STATS STRIP */

.stats-strip {

  display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);

  gap: 16px; margin-bottom: 32px;

}

.stat-card {

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 20px 20px 16px;

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm);

  transition: transform var(--trans), box-shadow var(--trans);

}

.stat-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md); }

.stat-card .sc-icon {

  font-size: 1.4rem; margin-bottom: 8px;

}

.stat-card .sc-val {

  font-size: 1.8rem; font-weight: 800;

  color: var(--c-text); line-height: 1;

  font-family: var(--font-display);

}

.stat-card .sc-label {

  font-size: .75rem; color: var(--c-text3); margin-top: 4px;

  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em;

}

.stat-card .sc-bar { margin-top: 10px; }

/* SECTION CARDS */

.section-heading {

  font-size: .7rem; font-weight: 700;

  letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase;

  color: var(--c-text3); margin-bottom: 16px;

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px;

}

.section-heading::after {

  content: ''; flex: 1; height: 1px; background: var(--c-border);

}

.unit-grid {

  display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr));

  gap: 14px; margin-bottom: 36px;

}

.unit-card {

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 18px;

  cursor: pointer; transition: all var(--trans);

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); position: relative;

  display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px;

}

.unit-card:hover {

  border-color: var(--c-accent); box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);

  transform: translateY(-2px);

}

.unit-card.completed { border-color: var(--c-success); }

.unit-card.completed::after {

  content: '✓'; position: absolute; top: 12px; right: 12px;

  width: 20px; height: 20px; border-radius: 50%;

  background: var(--c-success); color: #fff;

  font-size: .65rem; font-weight: 700;

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;

}

.unit-card .uc-num {

  font-size: .7rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--c-accent);

  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .05em;

}

.unit-card .uc-title {

  font-size: .9rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c-text);

  line-height: 1.35;

}

.unit-card .uc-cat {

  font-size: .72rem; color: var(--c-text3);

}

.unit-card .uc-tags {

  display: flex; gap: 4px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-top: 2px;

}

.tag {

  font-size: .65rem; font-weight: 600;

  padding: 2px 7px; border-radius: 99px;

  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .04em;

}

.tag-tense   { background: #EBF4FF; color: #2B6CB0; }

.tag-modal   { background: #FAF0FF; color: #6B46C1; }

.tag-passive { background: #FFF5F0; color: #C05621; }

.tag-clause  { background: #F0FFF4; color: #276749; }

.tag-article { background: #FFFFF0; color: #B7791F; }

.tag-preposition { background: #FFF0F0; color: #C53030; }

.tag-verb    { background: #E6FFFA; color: #2C7A7B; }

.tag-noun    { background: #F0F4FF; color: #3C366B; }

.tag-adj     { background: #FFF5FF; color: #702459; }

.tag-adv     { background: #FFFBF0; color: #975A16; }

.tag-phrasal { background: #F0F8FF; color: #2C5282; }

.tag-conj    { background: #F5F0FF; color: #553C9A; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-tense   { background: #1A2A3E; color: #90CDF4; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-modal   { background: #261733; color: #D6BCFA; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-passive { background: #2D1A10; color: #FBD38D; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-clause  { background: #1A2E22; color: #9AE6B4; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-article { background: #2D2510; color: #FAF089; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-preposition { background: #2D1010; color: #FEB2B2; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-verb    { background: #1A2E2E; color: #81E6D9; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-noun    { background: #1A1E3A; color: #B794F4; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-adj     { background: #2D1828; color: #FBB6CE; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-adv     { background: #2D2010; color: #FEFCBF; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-phrasal { background: #1A2030; color: #90CDF4; }

[data-theme="dark"] .tag-conj    { background: #201030; color: #C4B5FD; }

/* ============================================================

   UNIT LESSON VIEW

   ============================================================ */

#view-unit { display: none; }

.unit-layout {

  display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 380px;

  gap: 0; min-height: calc(100vh - var(--header-h));

}

.lesson-panel {

  padding: 40px 40px 80px;

  border-right: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  overflow-y: auto; max-height: calc(100vh - var(--header-h));

}

.exercise-panel {

  padding: 30px 30px 80px;

  overflow-y: auto; max-height: calc(100vh - var(--header-h));

  background: var(--c-surface2);

}

/* UNIT HEADER */

.unit-hdr {

  margin-bottom: 28px; padding-bottom: 20px;

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

}

.unit-hdr .uh-top {

  display: flex; align-items: flex-start; justify-content: space-between;

  gap: 12px; margin-bottom: 8px;

}

.unit-hdr .uh-num {

  font-size: .75rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .06em;

  text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--c-accent);

  background: var(--c-accent-light); padding: 3px 10px;

  border-radius: 99px;

}

.unit-hdr h2 {

  font-family: var(--font-display); font-size: 1.65rem;

  font-weight: 700; color: var(--c-text); line-height: 1.3;

  margin-bottom: 6px;

}

.unit-hdr .uh-meta {

  display: flex; gap: 8px; align-items: center; flex-wrap: wrap;

}

.unit-hdr .uh-actions {

  display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-shrink: 0;

}

/* LESSON CONTENT */

.lesson-section { margin-bottom: 28px; }

.lesson-section:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }

.ls-title {

  font-size: .7rem; font-weight: 700;

  letter-spacing: .08em; text-transform: uppercase;

  color: var(--c-text3); margin-bottom: 10px;

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;

}

.ls-title::before {

  content: ''; width: 3px; height: 14px;

  border-radius: 2px;

}

.ls-title.t-rule::before    { background: var(--c-accent); }

.ls-title.t-example::before { background: var(--c-accent2); }

.ls-title.t-note::before    { background: var(--c-warn); }

.ls-title.t-mistake::before { background: var(--c-error); }

.ls-title.t-compare::before { background: var(--c-gold); }

.ls-title.t-summary::before { background: var(--c-text3); }

.rule-list { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; }

.rule-item {

  background: var(--c-accent-light); border-left: 3px solid var(--c-accent);

  border-radius: 0 var(--r-sm) var(--r-sm) 0;

  padding: 10px 14px; font-size: .9rem;

  color: var(--c-text); line-height: 1.5;

}

.rule-item strong { color: var(--c-accent); }

.example-list { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; }

.ex-item {

  display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: flex-start;

  font-size: .9rem; line-height: 1.55;

}

.ex-item .ex-marker {

  color: var(--c-accent2); font-weight: 700; font-size: .85rem;

  min-width: 14px; margin-top: 2px;

}

.ex-item .ex-text { color: var(--c-text); }

.ex-item .ex-text em {

  font-style: normal; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c-accent);

  border-bottom: 1px dashed var(--c-accent);

}

.ex-item .ex-note {

  font-size: .78rem; color: var(--c-text3);

  margin-left: 4px;

}

.note-box {

  background: var(--c-warn-light); border: 1px solid var(--c-warn);

  border-radius: var(--r-md); padding: 14px 16px;

  font-size: .875rem; line-height: 1.55; color: var(--c-text);

  display: flex; gap: 10px;

}

.note-box .nb-icon { font-size: 1rem; flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 1px; }

.note-box strong { color: var(--c-warn); }

.mistake-box {

  background: var(--c-error-light); border: 1px solid var(--c-error);

  border-radius: var(--r-md); padding: 14px 16px;

  font-size: .875rem; line-height: 1.55;

}

.mistake-row {

  display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: center; padding: 4px 0;

}

.mistake-row .mr-x { color: var(--c-error); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1rem; }

.mistake-row .mr-check { color: var(--c-success); font-weight: 700; font-size: 1rem; }

.mistake-row .mr-text { font-size: .875rem; color: var(--c-text); }

.mistake-row .mr-wrong { text-decoration: line-through; color: var(--c-error); }

/* COMPARISON TABLE */

.compare-table {

  width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse;

  border-radius: var(--r-md); overflow: hidden;

  border: 1px solid var(--c-border); font-size: .875rem;

}

.compare-table th {

  background: var(--c-gold-light); color: var(--c-gold);

  padding: 10px 14px; text-align: left;

  font-size: .75rem; font-weight: 700;

  letter-spacing: .05em; text-transform: uppercase;

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

}

.compare-table td {

  padding: 9px 14px; vertical-align: top;

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  color: var(--c-text); line-height: 1.5;

}

.compare-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }

.compare-table tr:nth-child(even) td { background: var(--c-surface2); }

/* FORMULA BOX */

.formula-box {

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-md); padding: 14px 18px;

  font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: .875rem;

  color: var(--c-text); line-height: 1.7;

}

.formula-box .fb-sub { color: var(--c-text3); font-family: var(--font-body); }

.formula-box strong { color: var(--c-accent); }

.formula-box em { color: var(--c-accent2); font-style: normal; }

/* SUMMARY BOX */

.summary-box {

  background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--c-accent-light), var(--c-accent2-light));

  border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 18px;

  margin-top: 8px;

}

.summary-box h4 {

  font-size: .8rem; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase;

  letter-spacing: .06em; color: var(--c-text3); margin-bottom: 10px;

}

.summary-grid {

  display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;

  gap: 8px;

}

.summary-cell {

  background: rgba(255,255,255,.5); border-radius: var(--r-sm);

  padding: 8px 12px; font-size: .83rem;

}

[data-theme="dark"] .summary-cell { background: rgba(0,0,0,.2); }

.summary-cell .sc-form { font-weight: 700; color: var(--c-accent); font-size: .8rem; }

.summary-cell .sc-use  { font-size: .78rem; color: var(--c-text2); margin-top: 2px; }

/* COLLAPSIBLE */

.collapsible {

  border: 1px solid var(--c-border); border-radius: var(--r-md);

  overflow: hidden; margin-bottom: 8px;

}

.coll-header {

  padding: 11px 14px; cursor: pointer;

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;

  background: var(--c-surface2); font-size: .875rem; font-weight: 600;

  color: var(--c-text); user-select: none;

  transition: background var(--trans);

}

.coll-header:hover { background: var(--c-border); }

.coll-header .coll-arrow { transition: transform var(--trans); font-size: .75rem; }

.coll-header.open .coll-arrow { transform: rotate(90deg); }

.coll-body { display: none; padding: 14px; background: var(--c-surface); }

.coll-body.open { display: block; }

/* ============================================================

   EXERCISES

   ============================================================ */

.ex-panel-title {

  font-size: .75rem; font-weight: 700;

  letter-spacing: .07em; text-transform: uppercase;

  color: var(--c-text3); margin-bottom: 14px;

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;

}

.ex-panel-title .ep-score {

  font-size: .7rem; background: var(--c-surface);

  border: 1px solid var(--c-border); padding: 2px 8px;

  border-radius: 99px; color: var(--c-text2);

}

.quiz-card {

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 18px; margin-bottom: 12px;

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm);

}

.qc-num {

  font-size: .7rem; font-weight: 700; color: var(--c-text3);

  margin-bottom: 8px;

}

.qc-question {

  font-size: .9rem; color: var(--c-text); line-height: 1.55;

  margin-bottom: 12px; font-weight: 500;

}

.qc-question .blank {

  display: inline-block; min-width: 80px; height: 22px;

  border-bottom: 2px solid var(--c-accent);

  vertical-align: bottom; text-align: center;

  background: var(--c-accent-light); border-radius: 3px 3px 0 0;

  padding: 0 4px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c-accent);

}

.qc-options { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 7px; }

.qc-option {

  padding: 9px 13px; border-radius: var(--r-md);

  border: 1.5px solid var(--c-border);

  font-size: .86rem; cursor: pointer; color: var(--c-text);

  background: var(--c-surface2);

  transition: all var(--trans); text-align: left;

  line-height: 1.4;

}

.qc-option:hover:not(:disabled) {

  border-color: var(--c-accent); background: var(--c-accent-light);

}

.qc-option.selected {

  border-color: var(--c-accent); background: var(--c-accent-light);

  color: var(--c-accent);

}

.qc-option.correct {

  border-color: var(--c-success); background: var(--c-success-light);

  color: var(--c-success);

}

.qc-option.wrong {

  border-color: var(--c-error); background: var(--c-error-light);

  color: var(--c-error);

}

.qc-option:disabled { cursor: default; }

.fill-input {

  width: 100%; padding: 8px 12px;

  background: var(--c-surface2); border: 1.5px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-md); font-size: .875rem; color: var(--c-text);

  outline: none; transition: border-color var(--trans), box-shadow var(--trans);

}

.fill-input:focus {

  border-color: var(--c-accent);

  box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--c-accent-light);

}

.fill-input.correct { border-color: var(--c-success); background: var(--c-success-light); }

.fill-input.wrong   { border-color: var(--c-error);   background: var(--c-error-light);   }

.qc-feedback {

  margin-top: 10px; padding: 10px 12px;

  border-radius: var(--r-md); font-size: .825rem;

  line-height: 1.5; display: none;

}

.qc-feedback.show { display: block; }

.qc-feedback.correct {

  background: var(--c-success-light); color: var(--c-success);

  border: 1px solid var(--c-success);

}

.qc-feedback.wrong {

  background: var(--c-error-light); color: var(--c-error);

  border: 1px solid var(--c-error);

}

.qc-feedback .fb-answer { font-weight: 700; }

.ex-actions {

  display: flex; gap: 8px; margin-top: 16px; flex-wrap: wrap;

}

/* SCORE DISPLAY */

.score-display {

  text-align: center; padding: 20px;

  background: var(--c-surface); border-radius: var(--r-lg);

  border: 1px solid var(--c-border); margin-top: 16px;

}

.sd-number {

  font-size: 3rem; font-weight: 800;

  font-family: var(--font-display); line-height: 1;

}

.sd-number.good  { color: var(--c-success); }

.sd-number.ok    { color: var(--c-warn); }

.sd-number.poor  { color: var(--c-error); }

.sd-label { font-size: .875rem; color: var(--c-text2); margin-top: 4px; }

.sd-bar { margin: 12px auto 0; max-width: 200px; }

/* ============================================================

   EXAM MODE

   ============================================================ */

#view-exam { display: none; padding: 40px 48px; max-width: 880px; }

.exam-header {

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;

  margin-bottom: 28px; padding-bottom: 16px;

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

}

.exam-timer {

  font-size: 1.4rem; font-weight: 800;

  font-family: var(--font-display); color: var(--c-text);

}

.exam-timer.warning { color: var(--c-error); animation: pulse 1s infinite; }

@keyframes pulse { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: .5; } }

.exam-progress-strip {

  display: flex; gap: 3px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 20px;

}

.ep-dot {

  width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 50%;

  background: var(--c-border); transition: background var(--trans);

}

.ep-dot.answered { background: var(--c-accent); }

.ep-dot.current  { background: var(--c-warn); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--c-warn-light); }

.ep-dot.correct  { background: var(--c-success); }

.ep-dot.wrong    { background: var(--c-error); }

/* ============================================================

   BOOKMARKS / RECENTLY VIEWED

   ============================================================ */

#view-bookmarks { display: none; padding: 40px 48px; max-width: 900px; }

#view-recent    { display: none; padding: 40px 48px; max-width: 900px; }

#view-stats     { display: none; padding: 40px 48px; max-width: 900px; }

/* STATS VIEW */

.stats-grid {

  display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);

  gap: 20px; margin-bottom: 28px;

}

.stats-big-card {

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-lg); padding: 24px;

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); text-align: center;

}

.sbc-val {

  font-size: 2.5rem; font-weight: 800;

  font-family: var(--font-display); color: var(--c-accent); line-height: 1;

}

.sbc-label { font-size: .8rem; color: var(--c-text2); margin-top: 4px; }

.sbc-sub { font-size: .7rem; color: var(--c-text3); margin-top: 2px; }

.perf-table {

  width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse;

  background: var(--c-surface); border-radius: var(--r-lg);

  overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

}

.perf-table th {

  padding: 10px 14px; text-align: left;

  font-size: .72rem; font-weight: 700;

  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: .06em;

  color: var(--c-text3); background: var(--c-surface2);

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border);

}

.perf-table td {

  padding: 10px 14px; font-size: .875rem;

  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--c-border); color: var(--c-text);

}

.perf-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }

.perf-mini-bar {

  height: 6px; background: var(--c-border); border-radius: 99px;

  overflow: hidden; width: 80px; display: inline-block; vertical-align: middle;

  margin-left: 8px;

}

.perf-mini-fill { height: 100%; border-radius: 99px; }

/* ============================================================

   MODAL

   ============================================================ */

.modal-backdrop {

  display: none; position: fixed; inset: 0; z-index: 500;

  background: rgba(0,0,0,.5); backdrop-filter: blur(2px);

  align-items: center; justify-content: center;

}

.modal-backdrop.open { display: flex; }

.modal {

  background: var(--c-surface); border-radius: var(--r-xl);

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); padding: 32px;

  max-width: 520px; width: 90%; max-height: 90vh;

  overflow-y: auto; position: relative;

  animation: modalIn .25s ease;

}

@keyframes modalIn { from { opacity:0; transform: scale(.94) translateY(12px); } }

.modal-close {

  position: absolute; top: 16px; right: 16px;

  width: 28px; height: 28px; border-radius: 50%;

  background: var(--c-surface2); color: var(--c-text2);

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;

  font-size: .8rem; cursor: pointer; border: none;

  transition: background var(--trans);

}

.modal-close:hover { background: var(--c-border); }

.modal h3 { font-family: var(--font-display); font-size: 1.3rem; margin-bottom: 8px; }

.modal p { font-size: .9rem; color: var(--c-text2); margin-bottom: 16px; }

/* KEYBOARD SHORTCUT OVERLAY */

#shortcuts-modal .shortcut-grid {

  display: grid; grid-template-columns: auto 1fr;

  gap: 6px 16px; font-size: .875rem;

}

.sk-key {

  background: var(--c-surface2); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 8px;

  font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: .78rem;

  color: var(--c-text); white-space: nowrap;

  box-shadow: 0 2px 0 var(--c-border);

  justify-self: start; align-self: center;

}

.sk-desc { color: var(--c-text2); align-self: center; }

/* ============================================================

   TOAST NOTIFICATIONS

   ============================================================ */

#toast-area {

  position: fixed; bottom: 24px; right: 24px; z-index: 999;

  display: flex; flex-direction: column-reverse; gap: 8px;

}

.toast {

  background: var(--c-surface); border: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  border-radius: var(--r-md); padding: 12px 16px;

  box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg); font-size: .875rem;

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px;

  max-width: 320px; animation: toastIn .3s ease;

}

@keyframes toastIn { from { opacity:0; transform: translateX(20px); } }

.toast.success { border-color: var(--c-success); }

.toast.error   { border-color: var(--c-error); }

.toast.info    { border-color: var(--c-accent); }

.toast-icon { font-size: 1rem; }

.toast-msg  { flex: 1; color: var(--c-text); }

/* ============================================================

   NAVIGATION ARROWS

   ============================================================ */

.unit-nav-strip {

  display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;

  padding: 16px 0; border-top: 1px solid var(--c-border); margin-top: 24px;

}

.unav-btn {

  display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px;

  padding: 9px 16px; border-radius: var(--r-md);

  border: 1.5px solid var(--c-border); cursor: pointer;

  font-size: .83rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--c-text2);

  background: var(--c-surface); transition: all var(--trans);

}

.unav-btn:hover { border-color: var(--c-accent); color: var(--c-accent); background: var(--c-accent-light); }

.unav-btn:disabled { opacity: .35; cursor: not-allowed; }

.unav-btn:disabled:hover { border-color: var(--c-border); color: var(--c-text2); background: var(--c-surface); }

/* ============================================================

   CATEGORY FILTER TABS

   ============================================================ */

.filter-tabs {

  display: flex; gap: 6px; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 20px;

}

.filter-tab {

  padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 99px;

  font-size: .78rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer;

  border: 1.5px solid var(--c-border); color: var(--c-text2);

  background: var(--c-surface); transition: all var(--trans);

}

.filter-tab:hover { border-color: var(--c-accent); color: var(--c-accent); }

.filter-tab.active {

  background: var(--c-accent); color: #fff; border-color: var(--c-accent);

}

/* ============================================================

   PRINT STYLES

   ============================================================ */

@media print {

  #header, #sidebar, #reading-progress,

  .exercise-panel, .unit-nav-strip,

  .hdr-btn, #toast-area { display: none !important; }

  #main { margin-left: 0 !important; max-width: 100% !important; }

  .unit-layout { grid-template-columns: 1fr !important; }

  .lesson-panel { border: none !important; max-height: none !important; }

  body { background: #fff !important; color: #000 !important; }

}

/* ============================================================

   RESPONSIVE

   ============================================================ */

@media (max-width: 1100px) {

  .unit-layout { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }

  .exercise-panel {

    max-height: none; border-right: none;

    border-top: 1px solid var(--c-border);

  }

  .lesson-panel { max-height: none; border-right: none; }

}

@media (max-width: 900px) {

  :root { --sidebar-w: 0px; }

  #sidebar {

    transform: translateX(-300px);

    width: 300px; z-index: 200;

    box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg);

  }

  #sidebar.open { transform: translateX(0); }

  #sidebar-toggle { display: flex; }

  #reading-progress { left: 0; }

  #main { margin-left: 0; max-width: 100vw; }

  #view-home { padding: 24px 20px; }

  .stats-strip { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }

  .home-hero { padding: 28px 24px; }

  .home-hero h1 { font-size: 1.5rem; }

  #view-exam, #view-bookmarks, #view-recent, #view-stats { padding: 24px 20px; }

}

@media (max-width: 600px) {

  .stats-strip { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; }

  .stats-grid  { grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; }

  .unit-grid   { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }

  .summary-grid{ grid-template-columns: 1fr; }

  .lesson-panel { padding: 24px 20px 60px; }

  .exercise-panel { padding: 20px 16px 60px; }

  .home-hero::before { display: none; }

}

/* ============================================================

   MISC UTILS

   ============================================================ */

.hidden { display: none !important; }

.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); }

.text-accent  { color: var(--c-accent); }

.text-success { color: var(--c-success); }

.text-error   { color: var(--c-error); }

.text-warn    { color: var(--c-warn); }

.text-muted   { color: var(--c-text3); }

.fw-700 { font-weight: 700; }

.mt-8  { margin-top: 8px; }

.mt-16 { margin-top: 16px; }

.mb-8  { margin-bottom: 8px; }

.mb-16 { margin-bottom: 16px; }

.flex  { display: flex; }

.gap-8 { gap: 8px; }

.items-center { align-items: center; }

.justify-between { justify-content: space-between; }

</style>

</head>

<body>

<div id="app">

  <!-- HEADER -->

  <header id="header">

    <button class="hdr-btn" id="sidebar-toggle" onclick="toggleSidebar()" aria-label="Toggle menu">☰</button>

    <a href="#" class="logo" onclick="showView('home');return false;">

      <span>Grammar</span>InUse

      <span class="badge">4th Ed.</span>

    </a>

    <div id="search-wrap">

      <input id="search-input" type="search" placeholder="Search grammar topics… (press /)" autocomplete="off" />

      <span id="search-icon">🔍</span>

      <div id="search-results"></div>

    </div>

    <div id="header-actions">

      <button class="hdr-btn" onclick="showView('bookmarks')" title="Bookmarks (B)">🔖

        <span class="badge-count" id="bookmark-count" style="display:none"></span>

      </button>

      <button class="hdr-btn" onclick="showView('recent')" title="Recently viewed (R)">🕐</button>

      <button class="hdr-btn" onclick="showView('stats')" title="Study stats (S)">📊</button>

      <button class="hdr-btn" onclick="showView('exam')" title="Exam mode (E)">🎯</button>

      <button class="hdr-btn" id="theme-btn" onclick="toggleTheme()" title="Toggle dark mode (D)">🌙</button>

      <button class="hdr-btn" onclick="showShortcuts()" title="Keyboard shortcuts (?)">⌨️</button>

    </div>

  </header>

  <!-- READING PROGRESS BAR -->

  <div id="reading-progress"><div id="reading-progress-bar"></div></div>

  <!-- MAIN BODY -->

  <div id="body">

    <!-- SIDEBAR -->

    <nav id="sidebar">

      <div id="sidebar-inner" id="sidebar-inner">

        <!-- Injected by JS -->

      </div>

      <div id="sb-progress-wrap">

        <div class="sb-prog-label">

          <span>Overall Progress</span>

          <span id="sb-prog-pct">0%</span>

        </div>

        <div class="prog-bar-bg">

          <div class="prog-bar-fill" id="sb-prog-bar" style="width:0%"></div>

        </div>

      </div>

    </nav>

    <!-- MAIN -->

    <main id="main">

      <!-- ===== HOME VIEW ===== -->

      <div id="view-home">

        <div class="home-hero">

          <h1>Master English Grammar<br><em>Interactively</em></h1>

          <p>145 units from <em>English Grammar in Use</em> — explanations, examples, exercises, and quizzes, all in one place.</p>

          <div class="hero-actions">

            <button class="btn btn-primary" onclick="continueStudying()">▶ Continue Studying</button>

            <button class="btn btn-outline" onclick="showView('exam')">🎯 Exam Mode</button>

            <button class="btn btn-ghost" onclick="showView('stats')">📊 My Progress</button>

          </div>

        </div>

        <div class="stats-strip">

          <div class="stat-card">

            <div class="sc-icon">📖</div>

            <div class="sc-val" id="stat-studied">0</div>

            <div class="sc-label">Units Studied</div>

            <div class="sc-bar"><div class="prog-bar-bg"><div class="prog-bar-fill" id="stat-studied-bar" style="width:0%"></div></div></div>

          </div>

          <div class="stat-card">

            <div class="sc-icon">✅</div>

            <div class="sc-val" id="stat-completed">0</div>

            <div class="sc-label">Exercises Done</div>

          </div>

          <div class="stat-card">

            <div class="sc-icon">🎯</div>

            <div class="sc-val" id="stat-accuracy">—</div>

            <div class="sc-label">Avg. Accuracy</div>

          </div>

          <div class="stat-card">

            <div class="sc-icon">🔥</div>

            <div class="sc-val" id="stat-streak">0</div>

            <div class="sc-label">Day Streak</div>

          </div>

        </div>

        <!-- Category filters -->

        <div class="filter-tabs" id="home-filter-tabs"></div>

        <!-- Unit grid -->

        <div class="section-heading" id="home-grid-label">All 145 Units</div>

        <div class="unit-grid" id="home-unit-grid"></div>

      </div>

      <!-- ===== UNIT VIEW ===== -->

      <div id="view-unit">

        <div class="unit-layout">

          <div class="lesson-panel" id="lesson-panel"></div>

          <div class="exercise-panel" id="exercise-panel"></div>

        </div>

      </div>

      <!-- ===== EXAM VIEW ===== -->

      <div id="view-exam">

        <div class="exam-header">

          <div>

            <h2 style="font-family:var(--font-display);font-size:1.4rem;margin-bottom:4px">🎯 Exam Mode</h2>

            <p style="font-size:.85rem;color:var(--c-text2)">Test yourself across all grammar topics</p>

          </div>

          <div id="exam-timer" class="exam-timer hidden">00:00</div>

        </div>

        <div id="exam-setup">

          <div class="unit-grid" style="grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(220px,1fr))">

            <div class="unit-card" onclick="startExam('quick')">

              <div class="uc-num">⚡ Quick Test</div>

              <div class="uc-title">10 random questions</div>

              <div class="uc-cat">~5 minutes · All topics</div>

            </div>

            <div class="unit-card" onclick="startExam('standard')">

              <div class="uc-num">📝 Standard Test</div>

              <div class="uc-title">25 random questions</div>

              <div class="uc-cat">~15 minutes · All topics</div>

            </div>

            <div class="unit-card" onclick="startExam('full')">

              <div class="uc-num">🏆 Full Exam</div>

              <div class="uc-title">50 mixed questions</div>

              <div class="uc-cat">~30 minutes · All categories</div>

            </div>

            <div class="unit-card" onclick="startExam('weak')">

              <div class="uc-num">🎯 Weak Areas</div>

              <div class="uc-title">Focus on mistakes</div>

              <div class="uc-cat">Personalised · Your weak topics</div>

            </div>

          </div>

          <div id="exam-category-tests" style="margin-top:28px">

            <div class="section-heading">Category Tests</div>

            <div class="unit-grid" id="exam-cat-grid" style="grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fill,minmax(200px,1fr))"></div>

          </div>

        </div>

        <div id="exam-active" class="hidden">

          <div class="exam-progress-strip" id="exam-dots"></div>

          <div id="exam-questions"></div>

          <div class="ex-actions">

            <button class="btn btn-primary" id="exam-submit-btn" onclick="submitExam()">Submit Exam</button>

            <button class="btn btn-ghost" onclick="endExam()">✕ End Exam</button>

          </div>

        </div>

        <div id="exam-results" class="hidden"></div>

      </div>

      <!-- ===== BOOKMARKS VIEW ===== -->

      <div id="view-bookmarks">

        <h2 style="font-family:var(--font-display);font-size:1.5rem;margin-bottom:8px">🔖 Bookmarks</h2>

        <p style="color:var(--c-text2);font-size:.9rem;margin-bottom:24px">Your saved grammar units</p>

        <div class="unit-grid" id="bookmarks-grid"></div>

        <p id="bookmarks-empty" class="hidden" style="color:var(--c-text3);font-size:.9rem;text-align:center;padding:40px">No bookmarks yet. Click the 🔖 icon in any unit to save it.</p>

      </div>

      <!-- ===== RECENT VIEW ===== -->

      <div id="view-recent">

        <h2 style="font-family:var(--font-display);font-size:1.5rem;margin-bottom:8px">🕐 Recently Viewed</h2>

        <p style="color:var(--c-text2);font-size:.9rem;margin-bottom:24px">Units you've visited lately</p>

        <div class="unit-grid" id="recent-grid"></div>

        <p id="recent-empty" class="hidden" style="color:var(--c-text3);font-size:.9rem;text-align:center;padding:40px">No recently viewed units yet.</p>

      </div>

      <!-- ===== STATS VIEW ===== -->

      <div id="view-stats">

        <h2 style="font-family:var(--font-display);font-size:1.5rem;margin-bottom:8px">📊 Study Statistics</h2>

        <p style="color:var(--c-text2);font-size:.9rem;margin-bottom:24px">Track your learning progress</p>

        <div class="stats-grid" id="stats-big-grid"></div>

        <div class="section-heading">Performance by Category</div>

        <table class="perf-table" id="perf-table">

          <thead><tr><th>Category</th><th>Units Done</th><th>Accuracy</th><th>Progress</th></tr></thead>

          <tbody id="perf-tbody"></tbody>

        </table>

        <div style="margin-top:24px">

          <div class="section-heading">Unit-by-Unit Results</div>

          <table class="perf-table" id="unit-perf-table">

            <thead><tr><th>Unit</th><th>Title</th><th>Score</th><th>Attempts</th></tr></thead>

            <tbody id="unit-perf-tbody"></tbody>

          </table>

        </div>

        <div style="margin-top:24px;display:flex;gap:12px;">

          <button class="btn btn-ghost btn-sm" onclick="exportProgress()">📥 Export Progress</button>

          <button class="btn btn-danger btn-sm" onclick="resetProgress()">🗑 Reset All Progress</button>

        </div>

      </div>

    </main>

  </div>

</div>

<!-- MODAL BACKDROP -->

<div class="modal-backdrop" id="modal-backdrop">

  <div class="modal" id="modal-content">

    <button class="modal-close" onclick="closeModal()">✕</button>

    <div id="modal-body"></div>

  </div>

</div>

<!-- SHORTCUTS MODAL -->

<div class="modal-backdrop" id="shortcuts-modal">

  <div class="modal" id="shortcuts-modal-content">

    <button class="modal-close" onclick="document.getElementById('shortcuts-modal').classList.remove('open')">✕</button>

    <h3>⌨️ Keyboard Shortcuts</h3>

    <p>Navigate faster with these shortcuts:</p>

    <div class="shortcut-grid">

      <span class="sk-key">/</span><span class="sk-desc">Focus search</span>

      <span class="sk-key">Esc</span><span class="sk-desc">Close / back to home</span>

      <span class="sk-key">→</span><span class="sk-desc">Next unit</span>

      <span class="sk-key">←</span><span class="sk-desc">Previous unit</span>

      <span class="sk-key">D</span><span class="sk-desc">Toggle dark mode</span>

      <span class="sk-key">B</span><span class="sk-desc">Bookmark current unit</span>

      <span class="sk-key">E</span><span class="sk-desc">Open exam mode</span>

      <span class="sk-key">H</span><span class="sk-desc">Go to home</span>

      <span class="sk-key">S</span><span class="sk-desc">Open stats</span>

      <span class="sk-key">R</span><span class="sk-desc">Recently viewed</span>

      <span class="sk-key">P</span><span class="sk-desc">Print current unit</span>

      <span class="sk-key">?</span><span class="sk-desc">Show this panel</span>

    </div>

  </div>

</div>

<!-- TOAST AREA -->

<div id="toast-area"></div>

<script>

// ============================================================

// GRAMMAR DATA — All 145 Units (English Grammar in Use, 4th Ed.)

// ============================================================

const CATEGORIES = [

  { id: 'present-past',    label: 'Present & Past',          color: 'tense'   },

  { id: 'perf-past',       label: 'Present Perfect & Past',  color: 'tense'   },

  { id: 'future',          label: 'Future',                  color: 'tense'   },

  { id: 'modals',          label: 'Modals',                  color: 'modal'   },

  { id: 'if-wish',         label: 'If & Wish',               color: 'verb'    },

  { id: 'passive',         label: 'Passive',                 color: 'passive' },

  { id: 'reported',        label: 'Reported Speech',         color: 'verb'    },

  { id: 'questions',       label: 'Questions & Auxiliaries', color: 'verb'    },

  { id: 'ing-inf',         label: '-ing & Infinitives',      color: 'verb'    },

  { id: 'articles-nouns',  label: 'Articles & Nouns',        color: 'article' },

  { id: 'pronouns-det',    label: 'Pronouns & Determiners',  color: 'noun'    },

  { id: 'relative',        label: 'Relative Clauses',        color: 'clause'  },

  { id: 'adj-adv',         label: 'Adjectives & Adverbs',    color: 'adj'     },

  { id: 'conj-prep',       label: 'Conjunctions & Prepositions', color: 'conj' },

  { id: 'prepositions',    label: 'Prepositions',            color: 'preposition'},

  { id: 'phrasal',         label: 'Phrasal Verbs',           color: 'phrasal' },

];

const UNITS = [

  // ── PRESENT & PAST ──────────────────────────────────────────

  {

    id: 1, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Present continuous (I am doing)',

    subtitle: 'Actions happening now or around now',

    rules: [

      'Use <strong>am/is/are + -ing</strong> for actions happening at the moment of speaking.',

      'Also used for temporary situations and actions in progress around the present time.',

      'Use with <em>today, this week, this year</em> to describe current periods.',

      'Use with change verbs (get, become, grow, increase, rise, fall, improve) to describe ongoing changes.'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing\nI am working. / She is studying. / They are leaving.',

    examples: [

      { text: "Please don't make so much noise. <em>I'm trying</em> to work.", note: '(not: I try)' },

      { text: "'Where's Mark?' '<em>He's having</em> a shower.'", note: '(not: He has)' },

      { text: "<em>It isn't raining</em> any more. Let's go out." },

      { text: "Kate wants to work in Italy, so <em>she's learning</em> Italian." },

      { text: "<em>The population of the world is increasing</em> very fast." },

      { text: "A: You're working hard today. B: Yes, I have a lot to do." },

    ],

    notes: [

      'The action does not have to be happening at the exact moment of speaking. "I\'m reading a great book" — you may not be reading it right now, but you\'ve started and not finished.',

      '"I\'m always losing things" = it happens too often (expresses annoyance). Compare: "I always lose things" = every time, normal habit.'

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: 'I try to sleep. (right now)', right: 'I am trying to sleep.' },

      { wrong: 'She is having a car.', right: 'She has a car. (state verb)' },

      { wrong: 'Does your English getting better?', right: 'Is your English getting better?' }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Present Continuous', col2: 'Present Simple' },

      { col1: 'Action in progress NOW', col2: 'Habit / general truth' },

      { col1: "I'm eating lunch.", col2: 'I eat lunch at noon.' },

      { col1: "She's living in Paris (temp.)", col2: 'She lives in London (perm.)' }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 2, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Present simple (I do)',

    subtitle: 'Habits, facts, and permanent situations',

    rules: [

      'Use for things that happen <strong>regularly or repeatedly</strong>: habits, routines.',

      'Use for <strong>general truths and facts</strong> that are always true.',

      'Use for <strong>permanent situations</strong>.',

      'Use for performative verbs: <em>I promise, I suggest, I apologise</em> etc.',

      'Use <strong>do/does</strong> in questions and negatives. Add <strong>-s/-es</strong> for he/she/it.'

    ],

    formula: 'I/you/we/they + verb | he/she/it + verb-s/es\nNegative: don\'t / doesn\'t + verb\nQuestion: Do/Does + subject + verb?',

    examples: [

      { text: "Nurses <em>look after</em> patients in hospitals." },

      { text: "<em>The earth goes</em> round the sun." },

      { text: "I <em>usually go</em> away at weekends." },

      { text: "'What <em>do you do</em>?' 'I work in a shop.'" },

      { text: "What <em>does this word mean</em>?" },

      { text: "<em>I promise</em> I won't be late.", note: '(not: I am promising)' },

      { text: "Rice <em>doesn't grow</em> in cold climates." }

    ],

    notes: [

      'Do is also used as the main verb: "What do you do?" / "He doesn\'t do anything."',

      'Spelling: watch → watches, study → studies, go → goes, mix → mixes'

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: 'What means this word?', right: 'What does this word mean?' },

      { wrong: 'She works not here.', right: "She doesn't work here." },

      { wrong: "I'm promising I'll be good.", right: "I promise I'll be good." }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Use', col2: 'Example' },

      { col1: 'Habit', col2: 'I drink coffee every morning.' },

      { col1: 'General truth', col2: 'Water boils at 100°C.' },

      { col1: 'Permanent', col2: 'My parents live in Rome.' },

      { col1: 'Timetabled', col2: 'The train leaves at 6.' }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 3, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Present continuous and present simple 1',

    subtitle: 'I am doing vs. I do — key contrasts',

    rules: [

      'Continuous = action <strong>in progress now</strong> (not finished).',

      'Simple = <strong>general / repeated</strong> actions or permanent truths.',

      'Continuous for <strong>temporary</strong> situations; Simple for <strong>permanent</strong> ones.',

      '"I\'m always doing" = too often / annoyingly frequent (critical tone).'

    ],

    formula: 'Temporary → Continuous | Permanent → Simple',

    examples: [

      { text: "The water <em>is boiling</em>. Can you turn it off? (now)" },

      { text: "Water <em>boils</em> at 100°C. (general truth)" },

      { text: "<em>I'm living</em> with friends until I find a place. (temporary)" },

      { text: "My parents <em>live</em> in London. (permanent)" },

      { text: "You're <em>always playing</em> computer games! (too often — critical)" },

      { text: "I <em>always go</em> to work by car. (habit — neutral)" }

    ],

    notes: ['Some verbs are NOT used in continuous form: know, like, love, want, need, believe, understand, see, hear, seem, belong, contain, prefer.'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I'm knowing the answer.", right: "I know the answer." },

      { wrong: "She's liking this music.", right: "She likes this music." },

      { wrong: "You always complain! (mild habit)", right: "You're always complaining! (annoying habit)" }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Continuous (now/temporary)', col2: 'Simple (general/permanent)' },

      { col1: "I'm living here for now.", col2: 'I live in Bristol.' },

      { col1: "He's working late today.", col2: 'He works nine to five.' },

      { col1: "Why are you tasting the soup?", col2: 'This soup tastes great.' }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 4, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Present continuous and present simple 2',

    subtitle: 'State verbs and further contrasts',

    rules: [

      '<strong>State verbs</strong> describe a state, not an action — not usually used in continuous.',

      'Thinking verbs: <em>think, know, believe, understand, remember, suppose</em>.',

      'Feeling verbs: <em>like, love, hate, want, wish, prefer, need</em>.',

      'Perception: <em>see, hear, smell, taste, seem, appear, look</em>.',

      'Possession: <em>have, own, belong, contain, consist, include</em>.',

      'Some verbs change meaning: "I think you\'re right" (opinion) vs "I\'m thinking about it" (process).'

    ],

    formula: 'State verb + simple form (not continuous)\nAction verb → can use continuous',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I need</em> some water. (not: I'm needing)" },

      { text: "Do you <em>understand</em>? (not: Are you understanding?)" },

      { text: "<em>I think</em> you're right. (opinion = state)" },

      { text: "<em>I'm thinking</em> about changing jobs. (process = action)" },

      { text: "She <em>has</em> a new car. (possession = state)" },

      { text: "She <em>is having</em> lunch. (eating = action)" }

    ],

    notes: ['Look, smell, taste, feel can be used in continuous when they describe an action: "I\'m looking at the picture." vs "It looks beautiful."'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I'm wanting a coffee.", right: "I want a coffee." },

      { wrong: "He's owning three cars.", right: "He owns three cars." }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'State (simple)', col2: 'Action (continuous)' },

      { col1: "She has a car.", col2: "She's having a shower." },

      { col1: "I think it's true.", col2: "I'm thinking about it." },

      { col1: "This tastes good.", col2: "Why are you tasting it?" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 5, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Past simple (I did)',

    subtitle: 'Completed actions in the past',

    rules: [

      'Use for completed actions at a <strong>specific time</strong> in the past.',

      'Use for a <strong>series of past actions</strong>.',

      'Use for past <strong>habits and states</strong>.',

      'Regular verbs: add <strong>-ed</strong>. Many common verbs are irregular.',

      'Use <strong>did/didn\'t</strong> for questions and negatives.'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + verb (past form)\nDid + subject + base verb? | didn\'t + base verb',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>Mozart was</em> born in 1756. He <em>died</em> in 1791." },

      { text: "I <em>didn't sleep</em> well last night." },

      { text: "We <em>got up</em> early, <em>had</em> breakfast and <em>left</em>." },

      { text: "'<em>Did</em> you enjoy the film?' 'Yes, I <em>did</em>.'" },

      { text: "<em>It didn't rain</em> once during our holiday." }

    ],

    notes: ['Past simple is used with: yesterday, last week/month/year, ago, in 2010, when I was young.'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "Yesterday I have seen him.", right: "Yesterday I saw him." },

      { wrong: "Did you went to the party?", right: "Did you go to the party?" },

      { wrong: "I didn't went.", right: "I didn't go." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 6, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Past continuous (I was doing)',

    subtitle: 'Actions in progress at a past moment',

    rules: [

      'Use for an action <strong>in progress at a specific past time</strong>.',

      'Use for a <strong>background action interrupted</strong> by a shorter event (when + past simple).',

      'Use for two simultaneous past actions (while).',

      'Often sets the scene for a story.'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + was/were + verb-ing\nI was reading when the phone rang.',

    examples: [

      { text: "At eight o'clock last night, <em>I was watching</em> TV." },

      { text: "<em>I was walking</em> home when it started to rain." },

      { text: "<em>While she was cooking</em>, he was setting the table." },

      { text: "Tom hurt his back while <em>he was working</em> in the garden." },

      { text: "What <em>were you doing</em> at 11 last night?" }

    ],

    notes: ['Past continuous vs past simple: "He was reading when I arrived." (reading = ongoing background; arrived = shorter interruption).'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I was understand the question.", right: "I understood the question." },

      { wrong: "They was playing football.", right: "They were playing football." }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Past simple', col2: 'Past continuous' },

      { col1: 'Completed action', col2: 'In progress at a time' },

      { col1: 'I read the book.', col2: 'I was reading when she called.' },

      { col1: 'It rained all day.', col2: 'It was raining when I left.' }

    ]

  },

  // ── PRESENT PERFECT & PAST ───────────────────────────────────

  {

    id: 7, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Present perfect 1 (I have done)',

    subtitle: 'Past actions with present relevance',

    rules: [

      'Use for <strong>past actions with a present result or relevance</strong>.',

      'Use with <em>just, already, yet</em>.',

      'Use with <em>ever, never</em> for life experience.',

      'Do NOT use with finished time expressions (yesterday, last week, in 2010).'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + have/has + past participle\nI have done. / She has arrived.',

    examples: [

      { text: "I <em>have lost</em> my key. (I don't have it now)" },

      { text: "She <em>has just left</em>.", note: '(= a short time ago)' },

      { text: "<em>Have</em> you <em>ever been</em> to Australia?" },

      { text: "I <em>haven't seen</em> Tom lately." },

      { text: "Look — it <em>has stopped</em> raining!" }

    ],

    notes: ['Just = a short time ago. Already = sooner than expected. Yet = until now (in questions and negatives).'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I have seen him yesterday.", right: "I saw him yesterday." },

      { wrong: "Did you ever try sushi?", right: "Have you ever tried sushi?" }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Present Perfect', col2: 'Past Simple' },

      { col1: 'Present result', col2: 'Finished time' },

      { col1: "I've lost my key.", col2: "I lost it yesterday." },

      { col1: "Have you seen this?", col2: "Did you see the film last night?" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 8, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Present perfect 2 (I have done)',

    subtitle: 'With how much/many and superlatives',

    rules: [

      'Use after <strong>superlatives</strong> and with <em>first/second time</em>.',

      'Use for <strong>recent news or announcements</strong>.',

      'Use with "this is the first time", "this is the best X I\'ve ever..."',

    ],

    formula: 'This is the + superlative + I have ever seen.\nThis is the first time I have done this.',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>Scientists have discovered</em> a new planet." },

      { text: "This is the <em>best meal I've ever had</em>." },

      { text: "It's the <em>first time she's driven</em> a car." },

      { text: "The government <em>has decided</em> to cut taxes." },

      { text: "<em>How many times have you</em> been to Paris?" }

    ],

    notes: ['In American English, past simple is often used where British English uses present perfect: "Did you eat yet?" vs "Have you eaten yet?"'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "It was the best I ever ate.", right: "It's the best I've ever eaten." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 9, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Present perfect continuous (I have been doing)',

    subtitle: 'How long have you been doing something?',

    rules: [

      'Use for an activity that <strong>started in the past and is still continuing</strong>.',

      'Use to explain a <strong>present situation</strong> by describing a recent activity.',

      'Emphasises the <strong>duration or continuity</strong> of the activity.',

      'Use <em>for</em> (duration) or <em>since</em> (starting point).'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + have/has + been + verb-ing\nI have been waiting for an hour.',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I've been learning</em> English for six months." },

      { text: "<em>She's been working</em> here since 2019." },

      { text: "You look tired — <em>have you been running</em>?" },

      { text: "<em>It's been raining</em> since lunchtime." },

      { text: "My hands are dirty. <em>I've been fixing</em> the car." }

    ],

    notes: ['Some verbs are not used in continuous (know, like, believe). Use present perfect simple with these: "I\'ve known her for years."'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I've been knowing him for years.", right: "I've known him for years." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 10, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Present perfect continuous and simple',

    subtitle: 'I have been doing vs. I have done',

    rules: [

      'Continuous: emphasises the <strong>activity/process</strong> (how long, still ongoing).',

      'Simple: emphasises the <strong>result or completion</strong> (how many, what achieved).',

      'Both can be used with <em>how long</em>, but continuous is more common.',

      'Simple must be used when stating a <strong>number or quantity</strong>.'

    ],

    formula: 'How long → continuous preferred.\nHow many/much → simple required.',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I've been reading</em> this book for a week. (still reading)" },

      { text: "<em>I've read</em> 200 pages so far. (number = result)" },

      { text: "<em>She's been writing</em> letters all morning. (activity)" },

      { text: "<em>She's written</em> three letters. (quantity)" }

    ],

    notes: ['With "live" and "work", both forms are possible with "how long": "I\'ve lived here" / "I\'ve been living here"'],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Continuous', col2: 'Simple' },

      { col1: 'Process focus', col2: 'Result focus' },

      { col1: "I've been painting the house.", col2: "I've painted two rooms." },

      { col1: "She's been cooking.", col2: "She's cooked dinner." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 11, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'How long have you (been)...?',

    subtitle: 'Duration with present perfect',

    rules: [

      'Present perfect (not present) to ask/say <strong>how long something has been the case</strong>.',

      '"How long are you married?" is WRONG — use present perfect.',

      'For ongoing states: present perfect simple.',

      'For ongoing activities: present perfect continuous.'

    ],

    formula: 'How long + have/has + subject + (been) + verb?\nThey have been married for 20 years.',

    examples: [

      { text: "'How long <em>have they been</em> married?' 'For 20 years.'" },

      { text: "<em>Paul has been</em> in hospital since Monday." },

      { text: "<em>How long have you known</em> Jane?", note: '(not: How long do you know Jane?)' },

      { text: "<em>She's been waiting</em> all morning." }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "How long are you living here?", right: "How long have you been living here?" },

      { wrong: "They are married since July.", right: "They have been married since July." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 12, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'For and since / When...? and How long...?',

    subtitle: 'Expressing duration',

    rules: [

      '<strong>For</strong> = a period of time (for 2 hours, for years, for a long time).',

      '<strong>Since</strong> = a point in time (since Monday, since 2010, since I was born).',

      '"When did it start?" → past simple. "How long has it been?" → present perfect.',

      '<strong>For</strong> can be used with past simple too: "I lived there for 3 years."'

    ],

    formula: 'for + period: for six months / for a long time\nsince + point: since Monday / since 2015',

    examples: [

      { text: "I've lived here <em>for five years</em>. / <em>since 2019</em>." },

      { text: "<em>For how long</em> did they stay? — For a week." },

      { text: "<em>Since when</em> have you been learning Spanish? — Since last year." },

      { text: "She hasn't phoned <em>for ages</em>." }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I'm here since Monday.", right: "I've been here since Monday." },

      { wrong: "She's been ill since three days.", right: "She's been ill for three days." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 13, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Present perfect and past simple 1',

    subtitle: 'I have done vs. I did — the core difference',

    rules: [

      'Present perfect: the <strong>exact time is not stated</strong> or not important.',

      'Past simple: used with <strong>specific past time</strong> (yesterday, last year, in 2010).',

      'Present perfect: <strong>past experience up to now</strong>.',

      'Past simple: <strong>specific completed event</strong> in the past.'

    ],

    formula: 'Present perfect = past time unspecified / result now\nPast simple = specific finished time',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>Have you ever been</em> to Japan? (life experience so far)" },

      { text: "<em>Did you go</em> to Japan last year? (specific time)" },

      { text: "<em>I've broken</em> my leg. (result = I can't walk now)" },

      { text: "<em>I broke</em> my leg in 2018. (specific past event)" },

      { text: "'<em>Did</em> Shakespeare write Hamlet?' 'Yes, he did.'" }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I have been there last summer.", right: "I was there last summer." },

      { wrong: "Did you ever eat frog's legs?", right: "Have you ever eaten frog's legs?" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 14, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Present perfect and past simple 2',

    subtitle: 'News vs. details — shifting tenses',

    rules: [

      'We often use present perfect for <strong>news</strong>, then past simple for <strong>details</strong>.',

      '"I\'ve just heard..." (news) → "It happened when..." (detail, past simple).',

      'Use past simple for any specific who/where/when/how follow-up.'

    ],

    formula: 'News headline → present perfect\nDetails / follow-up → past simple',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>There's been</em> an accident. A car <em>hit</em> a cyclist." },

      { text: "<em>I've passed</em> my exam! I <em>got</em> 89%." },

      { text: "<em>Have you heard?</em> Dan <em>won</em> the lottery yesterday." },

      { text: "She <em>has left</em> her job. She <em>resigned</em> on Friday." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 15, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Past perfect (I had done)',

    subtitle: 'The past before the past',

    rules: [

      'Use to show that one past action happened <strong>before another past action</strong>.',

      'The earlier action = <strong>past perfect</strong>. The later action = past simple.',

      'Often used with: <em>when, after, because, already, just, by the time</em>.'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + had + past participle\nWhen I arrived, she had already left.',

    examples: [

      { text: "When I arrived, the film <em>had already started</em>." },

      { text: "She was tired because she <em>hadn't slept</em> well." },

      { text: "<em>Had</em> you ever driven abroad before that trip?" },

      { text: "He explained that he <em>had missed</em> the bus." },

      { text: "By the time we got there, they <em>had sold</em> all the tickets." }

    ],

    notes: ['If the sequence is clear (after, before), past perfect is optional: "After I left, she arrived." OR "After I had left, she arrived."'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "When I came home, she left already.", right: "When I came home, she had already left." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 16, cat: 'perf-past',

    title: 'Past perfect continuous (I had been doing)',

    subtitle: 'Duration before a past event',

    rules: [

      'Use to show <strong>how long an activity had been in progress</strong> before a past event.',

      'Emphasises the duration or continuity of the activity.',

      'Form: <strong>had been + verb-ing</strong>.'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + had been + verb-ing\nShe had been waiting for an hour when he arrived.',

    examples: [

      { text: "She <em>had been waiting</em> for an hour when he finally arrived." },

      { text: "He was out of breath — he <em>had been running</em>." },

      { text: "They were tired because they <em>had been travelling</em> all day." },

      { text: "<em>How long had you been learning</em> French before you moved there?" }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Past perfect simple', col2: 'Past perfect continuous' },

      { col1: 'Completion / result', col2: 'Duration / ongoing process' },

      { col1: "He had eaten.", col2: "He had been eating for hours." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 17, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Have and have got',

    subtitle: 'Expressing possession',

    rules: [

      '<strong>Have got</strong> = possession, relationships, illness (mainly British).',

      '<strong>Have</strong> (without got) = same meaning, used everywhere.',

      '"Have got" is NOT used in past (say "had", not "had got" for past).',

      '"Have" as an action verb (have breakfast, have a shower) — NOT replaceable with "have got".'

    ],

    formula: 'I have got / I have → possession\nDo you have? / Have you got? → questions',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I have/I've got</em> a new car." },

      { text: "<em>Do you have</em> a pen? / <em>Have you got</em> a pen?" },

      { text: "She <em>doesn't have / hasn't got</em> any brothers." },

      { text: "He <em>had</em> a beard last year.", note: '(not: had got)' },

      { text: "I <em>had</em> a good time. (action)", note: '(not: had got)' }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I had got a dog when I was young.", right: "I had a dog when I was young." },

      { wrong: "She has got a shower every morning.", right: "She has a shower every morning." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 18, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Used to (do)',

    subtitle: 'Past habits and states',

    rules: [

      'Use for <strong>past habits that no longer happen</strong>.',

      'Use for <strong>past states that are no longer true</strong>.',

      'Only exists in <strong>past</strong> — there is no "use to do" for present.',

      'Do NOT confuse with "be/get used to doing" (= be accustomed to).'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + used to + base verb\nI used to smoke, but I stopped.',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I used to smoke</em>, but I stopped two years ago." },

      { text: "She <em>used to live</em> in Paris.", note: '(she no longer lives there)' },

      { text: "<em>Did you use to</em> play a sport when you were young?" },

      { text: "There <em>used to be</em> a cinema here — now it's a car park." },

      { text: "I <em>didn't use to</em> like vegetables, but now I do." }

    ],

    notes: ['"I am used to getting up early" = it\'s normal for me now (be used to + -ing).\n"I used to get up early" = I did it in the past.'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I use to go there every day.", right: "I used to go there every day." },

      { wrong: "I am used to wake up early.", right: "I am used to waking up early." }

    ]

  },

  // ── FUTURE ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

  {

    id: 19, cat: 'future',

    title: 'Present tenses for the future',

    subtitle: 'Using present continuous and simple for future',

    rules: [

      'Present continuous = <strong>fixed future arrangement</strong> (plans with others).',

      'Present simple = <strong>timetable / schedule</strong> (trains, flights, programmes).',

      'Both suggest the event is already decided or fixed.'

    ],

    formula: 'Arrangement: Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing (tomorrow)\nTimetable: Subject + verb (time)',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I'm meeting</em> Sarah tomorrow evening. (arrangement)" },

      { text: "The train <em>leaves</em> at 9:30. (timetable)" },

      { text: "<em>We're having</em> a party on Saturday. Are you coming?" },

      { text: "The film <em>starts</em> at 7:30." },

      { text: "What <em>are you doing</em> this weekend?" }

    ],

    notes: ['"Going to" and "will" are also used for future — see Units 20–23.'],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "The bus is leaving at 6.", right: "The bus leaves at 6. (timetable)" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 20, cat: 'future',

    title: '(I\'m) going to (do)',

    subtitle: 'Intentions and predictions',

    rules: [

      '"Going to" = <strong>intention / plan decided before speaking</strong>.',

      '"Going to" = <strong>prediction based on present evidence</strong>.',

      'Contrast with "will" which is decided at the moment of speaking.'

    ],

    formula: 'Subject + am/is/are + going to + base verb',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I'm going to watch</em> the game tonight. (intention)" },

      { text: "Look at those clouds — <em>it's going to rain</em>! (evidence)" },

      { text: "She <em>is going to have</em> a baby. (she's pregnant)" },

      { text: "He said he <em>was going to</em> fix the car. (reported intention)" }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: '"Going to"', col2: '"Will"' },

      { col1: 'Pre-decided plan', col2: 'Decision at moment of speaking' },

      { col1: "I'm going to buy a car.", col2: "I'll have the fish, please." },

      { col1: 'Evidence prediction', col2: 'No visible evidence' }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 21, cat: 'future',

    title: 'Will/shall 1',

    subtitle: 'Predictions and decisions',

    rules: [

      '"Will" = <strong>prediction / expectation</strong> about the future.',

      '"Will" = <strong>spontaneous decision</strong> at the moment of speaking.',

      '"Will" = <strong>offers, promises, requests</strong>.',

      '"Shall I/we...?" = offer / suggestion (British English).'

    ],

    formula: 'will + base verb\nI will help you. / It will rain.',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I think it will rain</em> tomorrow." },

      { text: "A: We need milk. B: OK, <em>I'll get</em> some. (decision at moment)" },

      { text: "<em>I'll call</em> you when I arrive. (promise)" },

      { text: "<em>Shall I</em> open the window? (offer)" },

      { text: "<em>Will you</em> help me? (request)" }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "A: We need milk. B: OK, I'm going to get some.", right: "I'll get some. (spontaneous decision — use 'will')" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 22, cat: 'future',

    title: 'Will/shall 2',

    subtitle: '"I think I will..." and more uses',

    rules: [

      'Use "will" with: <em>I think, I expect, I hope, I\'m sure, probably, I doubt</em>.',

      '"Won\'t" = unwillingness or refusal (I won\'t do it).',

      '"Shall" for first person in offers/suggestions: Shall I? Shall we?'

    ],

    formula: 'I think/expect/hope + subject + will\nWon\'t = will not (refusal)',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>I think she'll</em> get the job." },

      { text: "I <em>expect</em> we <em>won't</em> be late." },

      { text: "<em>Probably</em> the concert <em>will be</em> cancelled." },

      { text: "She <em>won't listen</em> to anyone. (stubborn refusal)" },

      { text: "<em>Shall we</em> go out tonight?" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 23, cat: 'future',

    title: 'I will and I\'m going to',

    subtitle: 'Spontaneous vs. pre-planned',

    rules: [

      '"Will" = decision made <strong>now</strong> (at the moment of speaking).',

      '"Going to" = decision made <strong>before</strong> speaking.',

      'For predictions, both are possible; "going to" is used when there is present evidence.'

    ],

    formula: 'Spontaneous → will | Pre-planned → going to',

    examples: [

      { text: "A: Someone needs to fix this. B: <em>I'll do it</em>. (now-decision)" },

      { text: "A: Are you going out? B: Yes, <em>I'm going to buy</em> some shoes. (planned)" },

      { text: "'The sky is getting dark.' '<em>It's going to</em> rain.' (evidence)" },

      { text: "Hmm, I think <em>it will</em> rain later. (prediction)" }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: '"Will"', col2: '"Going to"' },

      { col1: 'Decided now', col2: 'Decided before' },

      { col1: "I'll answer the phone.", col2: "I'm going to call her back." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 24, cat: 'future',

    title: 'Will be doing and will have done',

    subtitle: 'Future continuous and future perfect',

    rules: [

      '"Will be doing" = action in progress at a <strong>future time</strong>.',

      '"Will have done" = action <strong>completed before</strong> a future time.',

      '"Will be doing" also used for polite enquiries (not imposing).'

    ],

    formula: 'will be + verb-ing → in progress\nwill have + past participle → completed by then',

    examples: [

      { text: "At 3pm tomorrow, <em>I'll be sitting</em> on a beach." },
