      { text: "<em>Carry on</em>! Don't stop. (on = continue)" },

      { text: "The match was <em>called off</em>. (cancelled)" },

      { text: "<em>Put on</em> your coat. (on = attached)" },

      { text: "She <em>put off</em> making the decision. (postponed)" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 112, cat: 'phrasal',

    title: 'Phrasal verbs 6–8 — up/down',

    subtitle: 'Increasing, decreasing, completing',

    rules: [

      '"Up" often = completion, increase, improvement.',

      '"Down" often = decrease, reduction, recording.',

      '"Give up" = stop trying. "Set up" = establish.',

      '"Write down" = record. "Let down" = disappoint.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>Give up</em> smoking." },

      { text: "She <em>set up</em> her own business." },

      { text: "<em>Write down</em> the main points." },

      { text: "He <em>let me down</em> — he didn't come." },

      { text: "The prices are <em>going up</em>." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 113, cat: 'phrasal',

    title: 'Phrasal verbs 9 — away/back',

    subtitle: 'Distance and return',

    rules: [

      '"Away" = distance, removal, continuation.',

      '"Back" = returning, reciprocation.',

      '"Go away" = leave. "Put away" = store.',

      '"Come back" = return. "Pay back" = repay.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "She <em>went away</em> for the weekend." },

      { text: "<em>Put away</em> your toys." },

      { text: "When are you <em>coming back</em>?" },

      { text: "I'll <em>pay you back</em> next week." }

    ]

  },

  // ── ADDITIONAL IMPORTANT UNITS ───────────────────────────────

  {

    id: 114, cat: 'ing-inf',

    title: '-ing and to-infinitive 3 (see someone do/doing)',

    subtitle: 'Verbs of perception',

    rules: [

      'Verbs of perception (see, hear, feel, watch, notice, smell) + object + <strong>base verb or -ing</strong>.',

      'Base verb = the complete action. -ing = action in progress.',

      '"Make/let" take object + base verb (no to).',

      '"Help" + object + base or to-infinitive (both possible).'

    ],

    formula: 'see/hear/watch + object + base verb (complete)\nsee/hear/watch + object + -ing (in progress)',

    examples: [

      { text: "I saw her <em>cross</em> the road. (complete action)" },

      { text: "I saw her <em>crossing</em> the road. (in progress)" },

      { text: "We heard them <em>singing</em>." },

      { text: "He made me <em>laugh</em>." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 115, cat: 'articles-nouns',

    title: 'Singular and plural',

    subtitle: 'Noun agreement',

    rules: [

      'Some nouns are always plural: <em>trousers, glasses, scissors, pyjamas, clothes</em>.',

      'Some look plural but are singular: <em>news, physics, economics</em>.',

      'Collective nouns (team, family, government) can take singular or plural.',

      'Irregular plurals: mouse→mice, child→children, woman→women, tooth→teeth.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>The news is</em> good. (not: are)" },

      { text: "My <em>trousers are</em> dirty. (always plural)" },

      { text: "<em>The team are</em> playing well. (British) / <em>The team is</em>. (American)" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 116, cat: 'articles-nouns',

    title: 'Noun + noun (a tennis ball / a headache)',

    subtitle: 'Compound nouns',

    rules: [

      'English often puts two nouns together: the first modifies the second.',

      'The first noun is usually singular, even if the idea is plural.',

      'Often written as one word, two words, or with a hyphen.',

      'Stress usually on the first word.'

    ],

    formula: 'noun + noun → first noun modifies second\na bus stop / a car park / a credit card',

    examples: [

      { text: "a <em>tennis</em> ball / a <em>shoe</em> shop" },

      { text: "the <em>city</em> centre / a <em>road</em> accident" },

      { text: "a <em>6-hour</em> journey / a <em>twenty-pound</em> note" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 117, cat: 'articles-nouns',

    title: '\'s (your sister\'s name) and of (the name of the book)',

    subtitle: 'Possession and association',

    rules: [

      'Use <strong>\'s</strong> for people, animals, organisations, and time expressions.',

      'Use <strong>of</strong> for things and objects.',

      'Plural nouns ending in -s: just add \'.',

      '"The city\'s streets" vs "the streets of the city" — both possible.'

    ],

    formula: 'person\'s/animal\'s + noun\nnoun + of + noun (for things)',

    examples: [

      { text: "It's <em>Tom's car</em>. / <em>The car's engine</em>." },

      { text: "<em>The name of the book</em>.", note: '(not the book\'s name)' },

      { text: "<em>Yesterday\'s</em> meeting. / <em>A three-hour</em> delay." },

      { text: "<em>The end of the story</em>." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 118, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'For, during and while',

    subtitle: 'Duration and time periods',

    rules: [

      '"For" = how long (a period of time): for two hours.',

      '"During" = within a period: during the film, during the summer.',

      '"While" = at the same time as (with clause): while I was sleeping.',

      '"For" vs "during": for = duration; during = when in a period.'

    ],

    formula: 'for + period of time\nduring + noun (the film)\nwhile + clause (while she was cooking)',

    examples: [

      { text: "I waited <em>for</em> two hours." },

      { text: "I fell asleep <em>during</em> the film." },

      { text: "I fell asleep <em>while</em> I was watching TV." },

      { text: "She worked there <em>for</em> three years <em>during</em> the 1990s." }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I met her during three months.", right: "I met her for three months." },

      { wrong: "I fell asleep while the film.", right: "I fell asleep during the film. (noun)" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 119, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'By and until / By the time',

    subtitle: 'Deadlines vs. duration',

    rules: [

      '"By" = not later than (deadline): by Friday = before or on Friday.',

      '"Until/till" = up to a point in time (duration): until Friday = keep doing until then.',

      '"By the time" = at the point when (followed by clause).'

    ],

    formula: 'by → deadline | until → continuation until a point',

    examples: [

      { text: "I'll be ready <em>by</em> 6. (no later than 6)" },

      { text: "I'll wait <em>until</em> 6. (from now until 6)" },

      { text: "<em>By the time</em> I arrived, she had left." },

      { text: "Can you finish this <em>by</em> the end of the day?" }

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: '"by"', col2: '"until"' },

      { col1: 'Deadline: finish before then', col2: 'Continue to that point' },

      { col1: "Finish by Monday.", col2: "Wait until Monday." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 120, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'At/on/in (time) — on time and in time',

    subtitle: 'Punctuality expressions',

    rules: [

      '"On time" = punctual, exactly at the planned time.',

      '"In time" = not too late, with time to spare.',

      '"At the end" = after everything has happened.',

      '"In the end" = finally, after a long time.'

    ],

    formula: 'on time = punctual | in time = not too late\nat the end = when something ends | in the end = finally',

    examples: [

      { text: "The train arrived <em>on time</em>.", note: '(= punctually, as scheduled)' },

      { text: "We arrived just <em>in time</em> for the concert.", note: '(= not late)' },

      { text: "<em>At the end</em> of the film, everyone clapped." },

      { text: "<em>In the end</em>, we decided to stay home." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 121, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'Still, yet and already',

    subtitle: 'Time adverbs',

    rules: [

      '"Still" = continuing, not stopped (often positive/negative statements).',

      '"Yet" = until now (in questions and negatives; expected).',

      '"Already" = sooner than expected (in positive statements).',

      '"Still" comes before the verb; "already" comes before the verb or at end; "yet" at end.'

    ],

    formula: 'still → continuing situation\nyet → until now (negatives/questions)\nalready → sooner than expected',

    examples: [

      { text: "She <em>still</em> lives at home. (continuing)" },

      { text: "Have you <em>already</em> eaten? / I've <em>already</em> seen this film." },

      { text: "I haven't finished <em>yet</em>. / Have they arrived <em>yet</em>?" },

      { text: "It's <em>still</em> raining!" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 122, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'Even',

    subtitle: '"Even" for emphasis',

    rules: [

      '"Even" emphasises something surprising or unexpected.',

      'Position: before the word it emphasises.',

      '"Even if" = despite the condition.',

      '"Even though" = despite the fact (stronger than although).'

    ],

    formula: 'even + comparative → even better, even more\neven if/though → concession with surprise',

    examples: [

      { text: "It was cheap — <em>even</em> I could afford it!" },

      { text: "He works every day, <em>even</em> on Sundays." },

      { text: "<em>Even if</em> I'm tired, I'll come." },

      { text: "<em>Even though</em> it was expensive, I bought it." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 123, cat: 'adj-adv',

    title: 'Word order 1 (verb + object; place and time)',

    subtitle: 'Basic English word order',

    rules: [

      'English order: Subject + Verb + Object + (Place) + (Time).',

      'Do NOT put an adverb between verb and object.',

      'Place before time (usually).',

      'Short adverbs of frequency go before the main verb.'

    ],

    formula: 'S + V + O + Place + Time\nShe bought a book at the shop yesterday.',

    examples: [

      { text: "I go to school by bus every day.", note: '(not: I go every day by bus to school)' },

      { text: "She <em>always</em> arrives early.", note: '(frequency adverb before main verb)' },

      { text: "He speaks English well.", note: '(not: He speaks well English)' }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "She likes very much coffee.", right: "She likes coffee very much." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 124, cat: 'adj-adv',

    title: 'Word order 2 (adverbs with the verb)',

    subtitle: 'Adverb position rules',

    rules: [

      'Adverbs of frequency: before the main verb, after "be".',

      '"Always/usually/often/sometimes/rarely/never/ever" go before main verb.',

      'After be: "She is always late."',

      'With auxiliary verbs: adverb goes between auxiliary and main verb.'

    ],

    formula: 'subject + frequency adverb + main verb\nsubject + be + frequency adverb',

    examples: [

      { text: "I <em>usually</em> get up at 7." },

      { text: "She <em>is always</em> on time." },

      { text: "He has <em>never</em> been to Japan." },

      { text: "They can <em>sometimes</em> be annoying." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 125, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'Although/though vs. in spite of/despite',

    subtitle: 'Contrast connectors — review',

    rules: [

      'Concession connectors link two contrasting ideas.',

      '"However/nevertheless/nonetheless" = but (connecting sentences).',

      '"On the other hand" = showing a contrasting viewpoint.',

      '"All the same / even so" = but still, despite what was said.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "The exam was hard. <em>However</em>, I passed." },

      { text: "I was tired. <em>Even so</em>, I went to the gym." },

      { text: "He's expensive. <em>All the same</em>, he's worth it." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 126, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'Like/as if/as though',

    subtitle: '"As if" for imaginary comparisons',

    rules: [

      '"As if/as though" = the way it would be if (often unreal).',

      'After "as if/as though", use past tense for present unreal situations.',

      '"Like" (informal) is sometimes used instead of "as if/as though".',

      'Use with act, behave, feel, look, seem, smell, sound, taste.'

    ],

    formula: 'look/feel/act as if/as though + clause\nHe acts as if he owned the place.',

    examples: [

      { text: "He talks <em>as if he knew</em> everything." },

      { text: "She looks <em>as though she's</em> seen a ghost." },

      { text: "I feel <em>as if</em> I'm floating." }

    ]

  },

  // Final Units

  {

    id: 127, cat: 'prepositions',

    title: 'Verb + preposition 2–5 (various)',

    subtitle: 'Common verb-preposition combinations',

    rules: [

      '"Depend on, rely on, insist on, congratulate on".',

      '"Think about/of, worry about, know about, dream of/about".',

      '"Blame for, forgive for, thank for, apologise for".',

      '"Interested in, involved in, succeed in, believe in".'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "It <em>depends on</em> the weather." },

      { text: "I'm <em>worried about</em> the exam." },

      { text: "She <em>apologised for</em> being late." },

      { text: "He <em>succeeded in</em> passing." },

      { text: "I <em>dreamed of</em> becoming famous." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 128, cat: 'prepositions',

    title: 'To/at/in/into',

    subtitle: 'Movement and direction prepositions',

    rules: [

      '"To" = movement towards a destination.',

      '"Into" = entering an enclosed space.',

      '"At" = aiming towards (throw at, look at, arrive at).',

      '"In" vs "to": "go to school" but "be in school".'

    ],

    formula: 'to → destination\ninto → entering\nat → location / direction',

    examples: [

      { text: "I'm going <em>to</em> the shop." },

      { text: "She walked <em>into</em> the room." },

      { text: "He threw the ball <em>at</em> the wall." },

      { text: "Don't look directly <em>at</em> the sun." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 129, cat: 'prepositions',

    title: 'At/in/on (other uses)',

    subtitle: 'Extended uses of place prepositions',

    rules: [

      '"At" = events: at a party, at a concert.',

      '"In" = media: in a newspaper, in a film, in a book.',

      '"On" = media/technology: on TV, on the internet, on the phone.',

      '"At" = home, work, school (specific places).'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "I saw it <em>on</em> television / <em>in</em> the newspaper." },

      { text: "She's <em>on</em> the phone right now." },

      { text: "I met her <em>at</em> a party." },

      { text: "I read about it <em>on</em> the internet." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 130, cat: 'questions',

    title: 'Negative questions / so do I / neither do I',

    subtitle: 'Agreement with negative and positive statements',

    rules: [

      '"So + auxiliary + I" = I agree with a positive statement.',

      '"Neither + auxiliary + I" = I agree with a negative statement.',

      '"Nor" = same as "neither" in this structure.',

      'These structures require the same tense/auxiliary as the original.'

    ],

    formula: '"I like it." → "So do I."\n"I don\'t like it." → "Neither do I." / "Nor do I."',

    examples: [

      { text: "'I love coffee.' '<em>So do I</em>!'" },

      { text: "'I can't swim.' '<em>Neither can I</em>.'" },

      { text: "'She was late.' '<em>So was</em> he.'" },

      { text: "'We haven't eaten.' '<em>Neither have we</em>.'" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 131, cat: 'conj-prep',

    title: 'Conjunctions: and/but/or/so/because',

    subtitle: 'Linking ideas with coordinating conjunctions',

    rules: [

      '"And" = addition. "But" = contrast. "Or" = alternative. "So" = result. "Because" = reason.',

      '"Because" = I explain why. "So" = therefore. "Because of" + noun (preposition).',

      '"Although" vs "because": although = contrast; because = reason.',

      '"Since/as" can replace "because" (slightly formal/informal).'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "I was tired, <em>so</em> I went to bed." },

      { text: "I went to bed <em>because</em> I was tired." },

      { text: "<em>Because of</em> the traffic, we were late." },

      { text: "<em>Since</em> she was busy, I left." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 132, cat: 'adj-adv',

    title: 'Quite, rather, fairly — further practice',

    subtitle: 'Boosters and softeners',

    notes: ['Use "rather" with both positive and negative adjectives; "quite" and "fairly" are usually neutral or positive.',

            '"Absolutely, completely, totally" are used with "extreme" adjectives: absolutely amazing, completely wrong.'],

    rules: [

      '"Absolutely/completely/totally" strengthen extreme adjectives.',

      '"Very" strengthens normal adjectives but NOT extreme ones.',

      '"Extreme" adjectives: amazing, awful, brilliant, terrible, exhausted, furious, tiny, enormous.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "It was <em>absolutely</em> amazing!", note: '(extreme adj)' },

      { text: "It was <em>very</em> good.", note: '(normal adj)' },

      { text: "She was <em>completely</em> exhausted." },

      { text: "This is <em>totally</em> wrong." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 133, cat: 'articles-nouns',

    title: 'The (3) — places revisited',

    subtitle: 'The with institutions and geography',

    rules: [

      'No article: continent/country names (Europe, France), lakes (Lake Geneva), mountains (Mount Everest).',

      'Use "the": rivers (the Thames), seas (the Pacific), mountain ranges (the Alps), groups of islands (the Canaries).',

      'Use "the" for regions: the Midlands, the Middle East.',

      'No article for most cities: Paris, New York (but The Hague).'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "She swam across <em>the English Channel</em>." },

      { text: "He climbed <em>Mount Kilimanjaro</em>. (no the)" },

      { text: "The longest river in Africa is <em>the Nile</em>." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 134, cat: 'questions',

    title: 'Exclamations: What...! / How...!',

    subtitle: 'Expressing strong feelings',

    rules: [

      '"What a/an + adj + noun!" or "What + noun!" (uncountable/plural)',

      '"How + adj/adv!" — more formal.',

      'No inversion in exclamations (different from questions).'

    ],

    formula: 'What a/an + adj + singular noun!\nWhat + noun! (uncountable/plural)\nHow + adj/adv!',

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>What a</em> beautiful day!" },

      { text: "<em>What</em> lovely weather!" },

      { text: "<em>How</em> strange!" },

      { text: "<em>What a</em> pity!" }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "How a beautiful day!", right: "What a beautiful day!" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 135, cat: 'ing-inf',

    title: 'To-infinitive with adjectives and nouns',

    subtitle: '"Nice to meet you" / "it\'s difficult to..."',

    rules: [

      '"It\'s + adj + to + infinitive": It\'s important to study.',

      '"It\'s + adj + of + person + to": It\'s kind of you to help.',

      '"The first/only/last + to + infinitive".',

      'Adjective + to + infinitive after nouns: a chance to win.'

    ],

    formula: 'It\'s + adj + (for someone) + to + base verb\nnoun + to + base verb',

    examples: [

      { text: "It's <em>difficult to</em> understand her." },

      { text: "It was <em>kind of you to</em> help." },

      { text: "She was the <em>first to arrive</em>." },

      { text: "This is a good <em>opportunity to</em> learn." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 136, cat: 'ing-inf',

    title: 'To...so as to / in order to',

    subtitle: 'Purpose with infinitive',

    rules: [

      '"To" + infinitive = purpose (simplest form).',

      '"In order to" / "so as to" = more formal versions.',

      '"In order not to" / "so as not to" = negative purpose.',

      'Do NOT use "for + -ing" for purpose of an action.'

    ],

    formula: 'to / in order to / so as to + base verb (purpose)',

    examples: [

      { text: "I studied hard <em>to pass</em> the exam." },

      { text: "She spoke quietly <em>in order not to</em> wake the baby." },

      { text: "He left early <em>so as to</em> avoid the traffic." }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "I went to the shop for buying bread.", right: "I went to the shop to buy bread." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 137, cat: 'phrasal',

    title: 'Phrasal verbs — mixed review',

    subtitle: 'Common phrasal verbs in context',

    rules: [

      'Phrasal verbs are very common in conversational English.',

      'They often have formal/single-word equivalents.',

      'Context determines meaning — many phrasal verbs have multiple meanings.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "<em>Look up</em> a word = search for it in a dictionary." },

      { text: "<em>Get on</em> = board (a bus); progress ('How are you getting on?')." },

      { text: "<em>Break down</em> = stop working (car); cry; analyse." },

      { text: "<em>Take off</em> = (plane) leave ground; (clothes) remove; become popular." },

      { text: "<em>Come across</em> = find by chance." }

    ]

  },

  // ── APPENDIX-RELATED UNITS ───────────────────────────────────

  {

    id: 138, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Irregular verbs (key examples)',

    subtitle: 'Most common irregular verb forms',

    rules: [

      'Irregular verbs must be memorised — they do not follow the regular -ed pattern.',

      'Three forms: base, past simple, past participle.',

      'Some verbs have all three forms the same: cut/cut/cut, put/put/put.',

      'Some have two forms the same: find/found/found.'

    ],

    formula: 'Base → Past Simple → Past Participle',

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Base', col2: 'Past Simple', col3: 'Past Participle' },

      { col1: 'go', col2: 'went', col3: 'gone' },

      { col1: 'do', col2: 'did', col3: 'done' },

      { col1: 'have', col2: 'had', col3: 'had' },

      { col1: 'see', col2: 'saw', col3: 'seen' },

      { col1: 'take', col2: 'took', col3: 'taken' },

      { col1: 'give', col2: 'gave', col3: 'given' },

      { col1: 'write', col2: 'wrote', col3: 'written' },

      { col1: 'speak', col2: 'spoke', col3: 'spoken' },

      { col1: 'know', col2: 'knew', col3: 'known' },

      { col1: 'think', col2: 'thought', col3: 'thought' },

      { col1: 'buy', col2: 'bought', col3: 'bought' },

      { col1: 'break', col2: 'broke', col3: 'broken' },

      { col1: 'come', col2: 'came', col3: 'come' },

      { col1: 'run', col2: 'ran', col3: 'run' },

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 139, cat: 'modals',

    title: 'Modal verbs — summary',

    subtitle: 'All modal verbs at a glance',

    rules: [

      'Modal verbs: can, could, will, would, shall, should, may, might, must, ought to.',

      'Modal verbs are followed by the base form (no to, except "ought to").',

      'They have no -s in third person: she can, he must.',

      'They have no tense changes — use "be able to, have to" for other tenses.'

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Modal', col2: 'Main uses' },

      { col1: 'can/could', col2: 'Ability, possibility, permission' },

      { col1: 'will/would', col2: 'Future, habit, conditional' },

      { col1: 'may/might', col2: 'Possibility, permission' },

      { col1: 'must', col2: 'Obligation, deduction' },

      { col1: 'should', col2: 'Advice, expectation' },

      { col1: 'shall', col2: 'Offers, suggestions (I/we)' },

      { col1: 'ought to', col2: 'Obligation/advice (like should)' },

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 140, cat: 'reported',

    title: 'Say and tell',

    subtitle: 'Key reporting verbs',

    rules: [

      '"Tell" always needs an object (tell someone): She told me the news.',

      '"Say" does not need an object: She said the news was good.',

      '"Tell" is used for: tell a story, tell a joke, tell the truth, tell a lie, tell the time.',

      '"Say" is used with direct speech: "Hello," she said.'

    ],

    formula: 'tell + person + (that) + clause\nsay + (that) + clause',

    examples: [

      { text: "She <em>told me</em> that she was leaving." },

      { text: "She <em>said</em> she was leaving." },

      { text: "He <em>told</em> a funny story." },

      { text: "'See you!' she <em>said</em>." }

    ],

    mistakes: [

      { wrong: "She said me the answer.", right: "She told me the answer." },

      { wrong: "He told that he was tired.", right: "He said he was tired. / He told me he was tired." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 141, cat: 'adj-adv',

    title: 'Negative prefixes (un-/in-/dis-/il-/ir-)',

    subtitle: 'Building negative adjectives',

    rules: [

      'un- + adj: unhappy, unfair, unusual, uncomfortable, unnecessary.',

      'in- + adj: incorrect, informal, incomplete, inconvenient.',

      'im- (before m/p): impossible, impatient, impractical.',

      'il- (before l): illegal, illegible.',

      'ir- (before r): irregular, irrelevant, irresponsible.',

      'dis-: disagree, dishonest, disappear, dislike.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "The test was <em>unfair</em>." },

      { text: "Parking here is <em>illegal</em>." },

      { text: "It's <em>impossible</em> to please everyone." },

      { text: "His handwriting is <em>illegible</em>." }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 142, cat: 'adj-adv',

    title: 'Adverbs: modifying adjectives and adverbs',

    subtitle: '"Very", "quite", "too", "extremely"',

    rules: [

      '"Very" = to a high degree (but NOT with extreme adjectives).',

      '"Absolutely/completely/totally/utterly" = extreme adjectives.',

      '"Rather/quite/fairly/pretty" = to some degree.',

      '"Too" = more than desired (negative). "Enough" = sufficient.'

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Normal adjective', col2: 'Extreme adjective' },

      { col1: 'good → very good', col2: 'excellent → absolutely excellent' },

      { col1: 'tired → very tired', col2: 'exhausted → absolutely exhausted' },

      { col1: 'cold → very cold', col2: 'freezing → absolutely freezing' },

      { col1: 'bad → very bad', col2: 'terrible → absolutely terrible' }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 143, cat: 'present-past',

    title: 'Spelling rules — verb forms',

    subtitle: 'When to double letters and use -ies',

    rules: [

      'Double final consonant (CVC pattern): stop→stopping, run→running.',

      'Do NOT double w/x/y: fix→fixing, play→playing.',

      'Verbs ending in -e: drop the e before -ing: come→coming, love→loving.',

      'Verbs ending in -ie: change to y before -ing: lie→lying, die→dying.',

      '-y → -ied in past: study→studied, try→tried (but play→played — vowel before y).'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "swim → swimming, sit → sitting, run → running" },

      { text: "make → making, ride → riding (drop the -e)" },

      { text: "study → studied, try → tried (-y → -ied)" },

      { text: "play → played (vowel before y → keep y)" }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 144, cat: 'questions',

    title: 'So/such/too/enough in context',

    subtitle: 'Mixed practice with degree expressions',

    rules: [

      'Summary of degree expressions: so, such, too, enough, very, quite, rather.',

      '"Such + a + noun" but "so + adjective" (without noun).',

      '"Too" = problem (too heavy to lift). "Very" = no problem implied.',

      '"Not ... enough" = below the required standard.'

    ],

    examples: [

      { text: "It was <em>such a</em> good film that I watched it twice." },

      { text: "It was <em>so</em> good that I watched it twice." },

      { text: "She's <em>too</em> young to drive." },

      { text: "She's <em>not old enough</em> to drive." },

      { text: "This coffee is <em>very</em> hot.", note: '(just a fact)' },

      { text: "This coffee is <em>too</em> hot.", note: '(I can\'t drink it yet)' }

    ]

  },

  {

    id: 145, cat: 'prepositions',

    title: 'Prepositions — comprehensive review',

    subtitle: 'All key prepositions at a glance',

    rules: [

      'Always check if a verb, noun, or adjective takes a fixed preposition.',

      'Common confusions: at/in for place (at the station vs in the city), for/since for time.',

      'No preposition after: mention, discuss, enter, contact, approach, reach, attend.',

      'Phrasal verbs: the particle is part of the verb — do not separate unnecessarily.'

    ],

    compare: [

      { col1: 'Time', col2: 'Place' },

      { col1: 'at (a point): at noon', col2: 'at (a point): at the door' },

      { col1: 'on (a day): on Monday', col2: 'on (a surface): on the table' },

      { col1: 'in (a period): in March', col2: 'in (an area): in Paris' },

      { col1: 'for (duration): for 3 years', col2: 'by (next to): by the river' },

      { col1: 'since (start): since 2020', col2: 'near: near the school' },

    ]

  },

];

// Map category ID to color class

const CAT_COLOR = {};

CATEGORIES.forEach(c => { CAT_COLOR[c.id] = c.color; });

</script>

<script>

// ============================================================

// EXERCISE DATA — Questions for every unit

// ============================================================

const EXERCISES = {

  1: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Right now, Sarah _____ in the car.", opts:["drive","drives","is driving","drove"], ans:2, exp:"Present continuous (am/is/are + -ing) for actions happening NOW." },

    { type:'mc', q:"Kate wants to work in Italy, so she _____ Italian.", opts:["learns","learned","is learning","has learned"], ans:2, exp:"Present continuous for an ongoing activity around the present time." },

    { type:'fill', q:"Please don't make so much noise. I ____________ (try) to work.", ans:"am trying", exp:"Present continuous: am/is/are + -ing." },

    { type:'fill', q:"It ____________ (not rain) any more. Let's go out!", ans:"isn't raining", exp:"Negative present continuous: isn't + -ing." },

    { type:'mc', q:"'Where's Mark?' '_____ a shower.'", opts:["He has","He's having","He has had","He have"], ans:1, exp:"He's having = He is having. Present continuous for now." },

    { type:'error', q:"The population of the world increases very fast right now.", ans:"is increasing", exp:"For ongoing changes, use present continuous: is increasing." }

  ],

  2: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Water _____ at 100 degrees Celsius.", opts:["is boiling","boils","boil","has boiled"], ans:1, exp:"General truth → present simple." },

    { type:'fill', q:"'What do you do?' 'I ____________ (work) in a shop.'", ans:"work", exp:"Present simple for permanent facts/jobs." },

    { type:'mc', q:"Rice _____ in cold climates.", opts:["doesn't grow","isn't growing","don't grow","not grows"], ans:0, exp:"Present simple negative: doesn't + base verb." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (promise) I won't be late.", ans:"promise", exp:"Performative verbs use present simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"'What _____ this word mean?'", opts:["does","is","do","means"], ans:0, exp:"Use 'does' for questions with he/she/it." },

    { type:'error', q:"She works not in this office.", ans:"She doesn't work in this office.", exp:"Present simple negative: doesn't + base verb." }

  ],

  3: [

    { type:'mc', q:"My parents _____ in London. They've lived there all their lives.", opts:["are living","live","lived","have lived"], ans:1, exp:"Permanent situation → present simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ with some friends until I find a flat.", opts:["live","am living","lived","have lived"], ans:1, exp:"Temporary situation → present continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"You're ____________ (always/play) computer games! (implies annoyance)", ans:"always playing", exp:"Always + present continuous = happening too often (critical tone)." },

    { type:'error', q:"I'm knowing the answer.", ans:"I know the answer.", exp:"'Know' is a state verb — not used in continuous." },

    { type:'mc', q:"The water _____. Can you turn it off?", opts:["boils","is boiling","boiled","has boiled"], ans:1, exp:"Action happening right now → continuous." }

  ],

  4: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Do you _____ what I mean?", opts:["understanding","are understanding","understand","understood"], ans:2, exp:"'Understand' is a state verb — no continuous form." },

    { type:'mc', q:"'Are you hungry?' 'Yes, I _____ about food.'", opts:["think","am thinking","thought","have thought"], ans:1, exp:"Thinking as an active process → continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She ____________ (have) a shower right now.", ans:"is having", exp:"'Have a shower' is an action, not a state → continuous." },

    { type:'error', q:"I'm wanting a coffee right now.", ans:"I want a coffee right now.", exp:"'Want' is a state verb — use simple form." },

    { type:'mc', q:"This soup _____ great!", opts:["is tasting","tastes","tasting","taste"], ans:1, exp:"State verb (perception) → simple." }

  ],

  5: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Mozart _____ in 1756.", opts:["is born","was born","has been born","born"], ans:1, exp:"Specific past event → past simple." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (not sleep) well last night.", ans:"didn't sleep", exp:"Past simple negative: didn't + base verb." },

    { type:'mc', q:"'_____ you enjoy the film?' 'Yes, I did.'", opts:["Have","Did","Do","Were"], ans:1, exp:"Past simple question: Did + subject + base verb?" },

    { type:'error', q:"Yesterday I have seen him.", ans:"Yesterday I saw him.", exp:"'Yesterday' = specific past time → past simple, not present perfect." },

    { type:'fill', q:"We ____________ (get up) early, had breakfast and left.", ans:"got up", exp:"Series of completed past actions → past simple." }

  ],

  6: [

    { type:'mc', q:"At eight o'clock last night, I _____ TV.", opts:["watched","was watching","have watched","watch"], ans:1, exp:"Action in progress at a specific past time → past continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (walk) home when it started to rain.", ans:"was walking", exp:"Background action (continuous) interrupted by a shorter event (simple)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"Tom hurt his back while _____ in the garden.", opts:["he worked","he works","he was working","he had worked"], ans:2, exp:"Simultaneous ongoing activity → past continuous with 'while'." },

    { type:'error', q:"They was playing football when I arrived.", ans:"They were playing football when I arrived.", exp:"Past continuous: was (I/he/she/it) / were (you/we/they)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"What ____________ (you/do) at 11 last night?", ans:"were you doing", exp:"Past continuous question: were + subject + -ing?" }

  ],

  7: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ my key. I can't get in.", opts:["lose","lost","have lost","had lost"], ans:2, exp:"Present result (I can't get in) → present perfect." },

    { type:'fill', q:"'Have you ever ____________ (be) to Australia?' 'No, never.'", ans:"been", exp:"Present perfect with 'ever' for life experience." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ just _____.", opts:["has/left","have/left","left/just","did/leave"], ans:0, exp:"Just + present perfect for a very recent action." },

    { type:'error', q:"I have seen him yesterday.", ans:"I saw him yesterday.", exp:"'Yesterday' is a specific past time — use past simple." },

    { type:'fill', q:"Look — it ____________ (stop) raining!", ans:"has stopped", exp:"Present result visible now → present perfect." }

  ],

  8: [

    { type:'mc', q:"This is the best meal I _____ ever _____.", opts:["have/eaten","had/eaten","did/eat","have/ate"], ans:0, exp:"Superlative + present perfect + ever." },

    { type:'fill', q:"It's the first time she ____________ (drive) a car.", ans:"has driven", exp:"'First time' + present perfect." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ scientists _____ a new planet?", opts:["Did/discover","Have/discovered","Has/discovered","Are/discovering"], ans:1, exp:"Recent news/discovery → present perfect." },

    { type:'error', q:"It was the best film I ever saw.", ans:"It's the best film I've ever seen.", exp:"Superlative with 'ever' → present perfect." }

  ],

  9: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ English for six months.", opts:["learn","learned","have been learning","am learning"], ans:2, exp:"Activity still continuing → present perfect continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"You look tired. ____________ (you/run)?", ans:"Have you been running", exp:"Present perfect continuous explains the present visible state." },

    { type:'mc', q:"It _____ since lunchtime.", opts:["is raining","rains","has been raining","rained"], ans:2, exp:"Activity started in past and still continuing → present perfect continuous." },

    { type:'error', q:"I've been knowing her for years.", ans:"I've known her for years.", exp:"'Know' is a state verb — use present perfect simple, not continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"My hands are dirty. I ____________ (fix) the car.", ans:"have been fixing", exp:"Recent activity explains present state → present perfect continuous." }

  ],

  10: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ 200 pages so far. (focus on quantity)", opts:["have been reading","read","have read","was reading"], ans:2, exp:"Number/quantity → present perfect simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ letters all morning. (focus on activity)", opts:["wrote","has written","has been writing","writes"], ans:2, exp:"Activity/process → present perfect continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"How long ____________ (you/live) here?", ans:"have you been living", exp:"Duration of ongoing activity → present perfect continuous." },

    { type:'error', q:"I've been writing three letters.", ans:"I've written three letters.", exp:"Number (three) → present perfect simple, not continuous." }

  ],

  11: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'How long _____ married?' 'For 20 years.'", opts:["are they","were they","have they been","did they be"], ans:2, exp:"How long + ongoing state → present perfect." },

    { type:'fill', q:"Paul ____________ (be) in hospital since Monday.", ans:"has been", exp:"Ongoing state since a point → present perfect." },

    { type:'error', q:"How long are you living here?", ans:"How long have you been living here?", exp:"How long + ongoing activity → present perfect continuous." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ this book for a week.", opts:["read","am reading","have been reading","was reading"], ans:2, exp:"Duration of ongoing activity → present perfect continuous." }

  ],

  12: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I've been waiting _____ an hour.", opts:["since","for","during","from"], ans:1, exp:"'For' + period of time." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She has lived here _____ 2015.", opts:["for","during","since","from"], ans:2, exp:"'Since' + point in time." },

    { type:'fill', q:"They've been married ____________ 25 years.", ans:"for", exp:"Period of time → 'for'." },

    { type:'error', q:"I'm here since Monday.", ans:"I've been here since Monday.", exp:"Ongoing state since Monday → present perfect." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She hasn't phoned _____ ages.", opts:["since","for","during","in"], ans:1, exp:"'For ages' = a long period of time." }

  ],

  13: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'_____ you ever _____ to Japan?' 'No, never.'", opts:["Did/go","Have/been","Have/gone","Did/been"], ans:1, exp:"Life experience up to now → present perfect with 'ever'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (break) my leg in 2018.", ans:"broke", exp:"Specific past time (2018) → past simple." },

    { type:'error', q:"Did you ever eat frog's legs?", ans:"Have you ever eaten frog's legs?", exp:"Life experience question → present perfect." },

    { type:'mc', q:"Did Shakespeare _____ Hamlet?", opts:["write","wrote","has written","written"], ans:0, exp:"Question about a completed historical action → past simple." }

  ],

  14: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'There's been an accident. A car _____ a cyclist.'", opts:["hits","has hit","hit","is hitting"], ans:2, exp:"Detail after 'there's been' → past simple." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (pass) my exam! I got 89%.", ans:"have passed", exp:"News → present perfect; detail (got 89%) → past simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ her job. She resigned on Friday.", opts:["has left","left","leaves","had left"], ans:0, exp:"Announcement/news → present perfect." }

  ],

  15: [

    { type:'mc', q:"When I arrived, the film _____ already _____.", opts:["has/started","had/started","was/starting","started/already"], ans:1, exp:"Earlier past action → past perfect." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She was tired because she ____________ (not sleep) well.", ans:"hadn't slept", exp:"Past perfect for reason before a past state." },

    { type:'error', q:"When I came home, she left already.", ans:"When I came home, she had already left.", exp:"Action before another past action → past perfect." },

    { type:'mc', q:"By the time we got there, they _____ all the tickets.", opts:["sold","have sold","had sold","were selling"], ans:2, exp:"'By the time' + past simple → past perfect for the earlier action." }

  ],

  16: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ for an hour when he finally arrived.", opts:["waited","has waited","had been waiting","was waiting"], ans:2, exp:"Duration before a past event → past perfect continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"He was out of breath — he ____________ (run).", ans:"had been running", exp:"Past perfect continuous explains a past state." },

    { type:'error', q:"They were tired because they travelled all day.", ans:"They were tired because they had been travelling all day.", exp:"Duration before a past result → past perfect continuous." }

  ],

  17: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'_____ you _____ a car?' 'Yes, I've got one.'", opts:["Do/have","Have/got","Did/get","Are/having"], ans:1, exp:"'Have you got' = do you possess? (British English)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ a dog when I was young.", ans:"had", exp:"Past possession → 'had' (not 'had got')." },

    { type:'error', q:"She has got a shower every morning.", ans:"She has a shower every morning.", exp:"'Have a shower' = action. Cannot use 'have got' for actions." }

  ],

  18: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ smoke, but I stopped two years ago.", opts:["use to","am used to","used to","was used to"], ans:2, exp:"Past habit that stopped → 'used to'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"There ____________ (use/be) a cinema here — now it's a car park.", ans:"used to be", exp:"Past state no longer true → 'used to be'." },

    { type:'error', q:"I use to go there every day.", ans:"I used to go there every day.", exp:"Past habit: 'used to' (not 'use to' in positive statements)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ getting up early. It's normal for me now.", opts:["used to","am used to","use to","would"], ans:1, exp:"Accustomed to = 'be used to + -ing'." }

  ],

  19: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ Sarah tomorrow evening. We arranged it last week.", opts:["meet","will meet","am meeting","would meet"], ans:2, exp:"Fixed future arrangement → present continuous." },

    { type:'fill', q:"The train ____________ (leave) at 9:30.", ans:"leaves", exp:"Timetabled event → present simple." },

    { type:'error', q:"The bus is leaving at 6 every morning.", ans:"The bus leaves at 6 every morning.", exp:"Timetable → present simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"What _____ this weekend?", opts:["do you do","will you do","are you doing","have you done"], ans:2, exp:"Future arrangement/plans → present continuous." }

  ],

  20: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Look at those clouds — it _____ rain!", opts:["will","is going to","is","would"], ans:1, exp:"Prediction based on present evidence → going to." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She ____________ (have) a baby. (She's pregnant.)", ans:"is going to have", exp:"Future based on present fact → going to." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I've decided. I _____ buy a new car.", opts:["will","am going to","would","shall"], ans:1, exp:"Pre-decided intention → going to." },

    { type:'error', q:"A: We need milk. B: I am going to get some. (spontaneous decision)", ans:"I'll get some.", exp:"Spontaneous decision made at the moment → 'will', not 'going to'." }

  ],

  21: [

    { type:'mc', q:"A: We have no milk. B: OK, I _____ get some.", opts:["am going to","will","would","shall"], ans:1, exp:"Spontaneous decision → will." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (call) you when I arrive. (promise)", ans:"will call", exp:"Promise → will." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ I open the window? (offer)", opts:["Will","Should","Shall","Would"], ans:2, exp:"'Shall I' = offer (British English)." },

    { type:'error', q:"I think it wills rain tomorrow.", ans:"I think it will rain tomorrow.", exp:"'Will' never takes -s: will (not wills)." }

  ],

  22: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ she'll get the job. She's very good.", opts:["think","am thinking","thought","will think"], ans:0, exp:"'I think + will' for future predictions/opinions." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ we go out tonight?", ans:"Shall", exp:"'Shall we' = suggestion (British English)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ listen to anyone. She's very stubborn.", opts:["won't","doesn't","isn't","can't"], ans:0, exp:"Won't = refusal (unwillingness)." }

  ],

  23: [

    { type:'mc', q:"A: Are you going out? B: Yes, I _____ buy some shoes. (planned before)", opts:["will","am going to","would","shall"], ans:1, exp:"Pre-planned intention → going to." },

    { type:'mc', q:"A: The phone is ringing. B: I _____ get it. (spontaneous)", opts:["am going to","would","will","shall"], ans:2, exp:"Spontaneous offer/decision → will." },

    { type:'error', q:"I'm going to answer the phone now. (spontaneous)", ans:"I'll answer the phone.", exp:"Spontaneous decision → 'will', not 'going to'." }

  ],

  24: [

    { type:'mc', q:"At 3pm tomorrow, I _____ on a beach.", opts:["will sit","will be sitting","am sitting","will have sat"], ans:1, exp:"Action in progress at a future time → will be + -ing." },

    { type:'fill', q:"By 10 o'clock, we ____________ (finish) dinner.", ans:"will have finished", exp:"Completed before a future point → will have + past participle." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ you be using the car tonight? (polite question)", opts:["Are","Will","Shall","Would"], ans:1, exp:"'Will you be doing' = polite future enquiry." }

  ],

  25: [

    { type:'mc', q:"When you _____, give me a call.", opts:["will arrive","arrive","arrived","are arriving"], ans:1, exp:"After 'when' in future clauses → present simple (not will)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"We'll go out when it ____________ (stop) raining.", ans:"stops", exp:"Future time clause with 'when' → present simple." },

    { type:'error', q:"When I will see him, I'll tell him.", ans:"When I see him, I'll tell him.", exp:"No 'will' after 'when' in future time clauses." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ it rains, we'll cancel the picnic.", opts:["When","While","If","Unless"], ans:2, exp:"'If' for possible/uncertain events; 'when' for certain ones." }

  ],

  26: [

    { type:'mc', q:"In the future, we _____ travel to Mars.", opts:["can","could","will be able to","are able to"], ans:2, exp:"Future ability → will be able to (not 'can')." },

    { type:'fill', q:"After months of practice, she ____________ (manage) to run 10km.", ans:"was able to", exp:"Specific past achievement → 'was able to' or 'managed to' (not 'could')." },

    { type:'error', q:"I could pass the exam last year. (specific success)", ans:"I was able to pass / I managed to pass the exam last year.", exp:"Specific one-time achievement in the past → 'was able to', not 'could'." },

    { type:'mc', q:"The word 'dream' _____ be a noun or a verb.", opts:["must","should","can","will"], ans:2, exp:"General possibility → can." }

  ],

  27: [

    { type:'mc', q:"You _____ been more careful. (past criticism)", opts:["could have","should have","can have","would"], ans:0, exp:"Could have done = had the ability but didn't use it." },

    { type:'fill', q:"That was dangerous! You ____________ (kill).", ans:"could have been killed", exp:"Past possibility of negative outcome → could have been + past participle." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ done it without your help.", opts:["couldn't have","can't have","mustn't have","wouldn't"], ans:0, exp:"It was impossible → couldn't have done." }

  ],

  28: [

    { type:'mc', q:"You've been travelling all day. You _____ tired.", opts:["can be","must be","should be","would be"], ans:1, exp:"Logical deduction (I'm sure it's true) → must." },

    { type:'fill', q:"That ____________ (be) Tom — he's in Paris.", ans:"can't be", exp:"Logical deduction (I'm sure it's NOT true) → can't be." },

    { type:'error', q:"He must forgot. He never answers his phone.", ans:"He must have forgotten.", exp:"Deduction about the past: must have + past participle." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She passed every exam. She _____ study very hard.", opts:["can","must","should","would"], ans:1, exp:"Logical deduction → must." }

  ],

  29: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ go to Paris next week. I'm not sure yet.", opts:["will","must","may","should"], ans:2, exp:"Possibility (not certain) → may/might." },

    { type:'fill', q:"It ____________ (rain) this afternoon. Take an umbrella.", ans:"might rain", exp:"Possible future event → might + base verb." },

    { type:'error', q:"He may to be late.", ans:"He may be late.", exp:"Modal verbs are followed by base verb (no 'to')." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ have missed the train.", opts:["might","must to","may to","should"], ans:0, exp:"Possibility about the past → might have + past participle." }

  ],

  30: [

    { type:'mc', q:"We've finished early. We _____ go home.", opts:["might as well","should","had better","will"], ans:0, exp:"No better option available → might as well." },

    { type:'fill', q:"There's nothing on TV. I ____________ go to bed.", ans:"might as well", exp:"'Might as well' = no better option." }

  ],

  31: [

    { type:'mc', q:"You _____ wear a tie. It's not necessary.", opts:["mustn't","don't have to","shouldn't","can't"], ans:1, exp:"'Don't have to' = not necessary (you have a choice)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"You ____________ tell anyone. It's a secret.", ans:"mustn't", exp:"'Mustn't' = forbidden/not allowed." },

    { type:'error', q:"I didn't have to went to work yesterday.", ans:"I didn't have to go to work yesterday.", exp:"After 'have to' → base verb (not past form)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ wait an hour for the bus. (past obligation)", opts:["must","has to","had to","should"], ans:2, exp:"Past obligation → had to (not must)." }

  ],

  32: [

    { type:'mc', q:"You _____ shout — I can hear you perfectly.", opts:["mustn't","needn't","shouldn't","don't have"], ans:1, exp:"'Needn't' = it's not necessary." },

    { type:'fill', q:"You ____________ have cooked — we were going out anyway.", ans:"needn't have", exp:"'Needn't have done' = did it but it was unnecessary." },

    { type:'mc', q:"You _____ park here. It's a no-parking zone.", opts:["don't have to","needn't","mustn't","shouldn't"], ans:2, exp:"'Mustn't' = prohibited/forbidden." }

  ],

  33: [

    { type:'mc', q:"You _____ see a doctor about that cough.", opts:["must","should","shall","have to"], ans:1, exp:"Advice/recommendation → should." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (study) more. I regret it now.", ans:"should have studied", exp:"Past regret/criticism: should have + past participle." },

    { type:'error', q:"I should to call him.", ans:"I should call him.", exp:"Modal verbs + base verb (no 'to' after 'should')." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ have left without saying goodbye.", opts:["shouldn't","mustn't","can't","wouldn't"], ans:0, exp:"Past criticism → shouldn't have done." }

  ],

  34: [

    { type:'mc', q:"It's strange that she _____ say that.", opts:["would","should","could","must"], ans:1, exp:"'Should' expresses surprise or unexpectedness." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I suggest that you ____________ (see) a lawyer.", ans:"should see", exp:"After 'suggest that' → should + base verb." }

  ],

  35: [

    { type:'mc', q:"You _____ take an umbrella — it looks like rain.", opts:["should","had better","must","have to"], ans:1, exp:"'Had better' = strong advice for specific situation." },

    { type:'fill', q:"It's 2am. It's time ____________ (go) to bed.", ans:"to go", exp:"'It's time to + infinitive' = overdue action." },

    { type:'error', q:"You had better to take an umbrella.", ans:"You had better take an umbrella.", exp:"'Had better' + base verb (no 'to')." },

    { type:'mc', q:"It's time you _____ a job.", opts:["get","got","will get","to get"], ans:1, exp:"'It's time + subject + past tense' = something is overdue." }

  ],

  36: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ open the door. She refused.", opts:["wouldn't","won't","can't","mustn't"], ans:0, exp:"'Wouldn't' = past refusal/unwillingness." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (rather) stay home than go out.", ans:"would rather", exp:"'Would rather + base verb' = preference." },

    { type:'error', q:"I would rather to stay.", ans:"I would rather stay.", exp:"'Would rather' + base verb (no 'to')." }

  ],

  37: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ you help me carry this? (polite request)", opts:["Will","Can","Could","Shall"], ans:2, exp:"'Could you' is more polite than 'can you'." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ I use your phone?", opts:["Would","Shall","May","Could I"], ans:2, exp:"'May I' = polite permission." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ I carry that for you? (offer)", ans:"Shall", exp:"'Shall I' = offer to help." }

  ],

  38: [

    { type:'mc', q:"If it _____, we'll cancel the match.", opts:["will rain","rains","rained","would rain"], ans:1, exp:"Type 1 conditional: if + present simple → will." },

    { type:'fill', q:"If I ____________ (have) more money, I'd travel.", ans:"had", exp:"Type 2 conditional: if + past simple → would." },

    { type:'error', q:"If I would have time, I would help.", ans:"If I had time, I would help.", exp:"No 'would' in the if-clause. Use past simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"What _____ you do if you lost your job?", opts:["will","would","should","could"], ans:1, exp:"Type 2 unreal conditional → would." }

  ],

  39: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I wish I _____ the answer. (I don't know it)", opts:["know","knew","have known","will know"], ans:1, exp:"Wish + past simple for present unreal wishes." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I wish I ____________ (speak) better French.", ans:"could speak", exp:"Wish + could + base verb for ability wishes." },

    { type:'error', q:"I wish I can speak Spanish.", ans:"I wish I could speak Spanish.", exp:"Wish + could (not can) for present ability wishes." },

    { type:'mc', q:"If I knew her number, I _____ call her.", opts:["will","would","should","might"], ans:1, exp:"Type 2 unreal conditional → would." }

  ],

  40: [

    { type:'mc', q:"If I had known you were ill, I _____ visited you.", opts:["will have","would have","had","could"], ans:1, exp:"Type 3 conditional: would have + past participle." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I wish I ____________ (study) harder. (past regret)", ans:"had studied", exp:"Wish + past perfect for past regrets." },

    { type:'error', q:"I wish I would have gone to the party.", ans:"I wish I had gone to the party.", exp:"Past regret: wish + past perfect (not 'would have')." }

  ],

  41: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I wish you _____ stop making that noise! (frustration)", opts:["would","will","could","should"], ans:0, exp:"Wish + would for wanting a change in behaviour." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I wish I ____________ (live) nearer the sea. (now wish)", ans:"lived", exp:"Present unreal wish → wish + past simple." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I wish I _____ taken that job. (past regret)", opts:["have","had","would have","could"], ans:1, exp:"Past regret → wish + past perfect." }

  ],

  42: [

    { type:'mc', q:"My car _____ last night.", opts:["stole","was stolen","has stolen","is stolen"], ans:1, exp:"Past passive: was/were + past participle." },

    { type:'fill', q:"These cars ____________ (make) in Japan.", ans:"are made", exp:"Present passive: am/is/are + past participle." },

    { type:'error', q:"The letter was sended last week.", ans:"The letter was sent last week.", exp:"'Send' is irregular: sent (not sended)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"English _____ all over the world.", opts:["speaks","is spoken","is speaking","spoke"], ans:1, exp:"General passive fact → is + past participle." }

  ],

  43: [

    { type:'mc', q:"The bridge _____ right now. (ongoing passive)", opts:["is repaired","is being repaired","has repaired","was repaired"], ans:1, exp:"Continuous passive: is/are being + past participle." },

    { type:'fill', q:"The house ____________ (paint). (recently completed)", ans:"has been painted", exp:"Perfect passive: has/have been + past participle." },

    { type:'mc', q:"The application _____ by Friday.", opts:["must submit","must be submitted","must have submitted","is submitting"], ans:1, exp:"Modal passive: must be + past participle." }

  ],

  44: [

    { type:'mc', q:"How did he _____?", opts:["get hurt","was hurt","got hurted","be hurt"], ans:0, exp:"Get passive (informal): get + past participle." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She ____________ (promote) last month.", ans:"got promoted", exp:"Get passive for informal/unexpected events." }

  ],

  45: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ that the suspect is hiding in France.", opts:["It says","It is said","It said","They say"], ans:1, exp:"Passive reporting: It is said that + clause." },

    { type:'fill', q:"The suspect ____________ (believe/hide) in France.", ans:"is believed to be hiding", exp:"Subject + is believed + to be + -ing." },

    { type:'mc', q:"He _____ to run ten miles a day.", opts:["says","is said","said","was saying"], ans:1, exp:"Subject + is said + to + infinitive." }

  ],

  46: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ my hair cut yesterday.", opts:["had","have","got","did"], ans:0, exp:"Causative have: had + object + past participle." },

    { type:'fill', q:"We're ____________ the house painted.", ans:"having", exp:"have something done: having + object + past participle." },

    { type:'error', q:"I had cut my hair.", ans:"I had my hair cut.", exp:"'Have something done' = arrange for someone else: had + object + past participle." }

  ],

  47: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'I am tired.' → She said she _____ tired.", opts:["is","was","has been","were"], ans:1, exp:"Present → past in reported speech." },

    { type:'fill', q:"'I will come.' → She said she ____________.", ans:"would come", exp:"Will → would in reported speech." },

    { type:'mc', q:"'I can swim.' → He said he _____ swim.", opts:["can","could","will","shall"], ans:1, exp:"Can → could in reported speech." },

    { type:'error', q:"She said me that she was leaving.", ans:"She told me that she was leaving.", exp:"'Tell' needs an object: told me. 'Say' doesn't: she said that." }

  ],

  48: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'Where do you live?' → She asked me where I _____.", opts:["live","lived","do live","did live"], ans:1, exp:"Reported question: normal word order, tense shift." },

    { type:'fill', q:"'Sit down!' → She ____________ me to sit down.", ans:"told", exp:"Reported command: told + object + to + infinitive." },

    { type:'error', q:"She asked me where did I live.", ans:"She asked me where I lived.", exp:"Reported questions use normal word order (no inversion)." }

  ],

  49: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'Who _____ you?' (Who is the subject)", opts:["did tell","tells","told","has told"], ans:2, exp:"When 'who' is the subject → no auxiliary needed." },

    { type:'fill', q:"'What ____________ this word mean?'", ans:"does", exp:"Auxiliary 'does' needed because 'this word' is the subject." },

    { type:'error', q:"What means this word?", ans:"What does this word mean?", exp:"'Mean' needs auxiliary: What does + subject + verb?" }

  ],

  50: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Do you know _____ the bank is?", opts:["where","where is","where does","where can"], ans:0, exp:"Indirect question: normal word order after 'where'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"Can you tell me what time it ____________?", ans:"starts", exp:"Indirect question: normal word order (no inversion)." },

    { type:'error', q:"Do you know where is the station?", ans:"Do you know where the station is?", exp:"Indirect questions use normal word order." }

  ],

  51: [

    { type:'mc', q:"'I love coffee.' '_____ do I!'", opts:["Neither","So","Too","Also"], ans:1, exp:"Agreeing with a positive statement: 'So do I'." },

    { type:'mc', q:"'I can't swim.' '_____ can I.'", opts:["So","Neither","Either","Also"], ans:1, exp:"Agreeing with a negative statement: 'Neither can I'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"'Is it raining?' 'I ____________.' (positive short answer)", ans:"think so", exp:"Positive short answer: 'I think so'." }

  ],

  52: [

    { type:'mc', q:"It's cold today, _____ it?", opts:["isn't","is","wasn't","was"], ans:0, exp:"Positive sentence → negative tag. 'It is' → 'isn't it'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She hasn't called, ____________?", ans:"has she", exp:"Negative sentence → positive tag. 'hasn't' → 'has she'." },

    { type:'error', q:"You like coffee, isn't it?", ans:"You like coffee, don't you?", exp:"Tag must use the same auxiliary as the clause: 'like' → 'do you'." }

  ],

  53: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Have you finished _____ the dishes?", opts:["to do","do","doing","done"], ans:2, exp:"'Finish' + -ing form." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I don't mind ____________ (wait).", ans:"waiting", exp:"'Mind' + -ing form." },

    { type:'error', q:"She denied to take the money.", ans:"She denied taking the money.", exp:"'Deny' + -ing form (not infinitive)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ smoking last year.", opts:["stopped to","stopped","stop","stops"], ans:1, exp:"'Stop smoking' = quit. 'Stop to smoke' = pause to have a cigarette." }

  ],

  54: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ to go home.", opts:["wanting","am wanting","want","have wanted"], ans:2, exp:"'Want' + to-infinitive (and it's a state verb — no continuous)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"He ____________ (manage) to open the door.", ans:"managed", exp:"'Manage' + to-infinitive." },

    { type:'error', q:"I want going home.", ans:"I want to go home.", exp:"'Want' + to + base verb (not -ing)." }

  ],

  55: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I remember _____ her before. (memory of past)", opts:["to meet","meet","meeting","met"], ans:2, exp:"Remember + -ing = memory of a past event." },

    { type:'mc', q:"Remember _____ her tonight! (don't forget)", opts:["calling","call","to call","called"], ans:2, exp:"Remember + to-infinitive = don't forget to do it." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I tried ____________ (lift) it but it was too heavy. (attempt)", ans:"to lift", exp:"Try + to-infinitive = attempt." },

    { type:'error', q:"I forgot locking the door. (I didn't do it)", ans:"I forgot to lock the door.", exp:"Forget + to-infinitive = fail to do something." }

  ],

  56: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ swimming. (general feeling)", opts:["like to swim","am liking swimming","like swimming","would like to swim"], ans:2, exp:"Like + -ing for general feelings." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ to swim today. (specific wish)", opts:["like","am liking","would like","likes"], ans:2, exp:"Would like + to-infinitive for specific desire." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (prefer) cycling to driving.", ans:"prefer", exp:"Prefer + -ing + to + -ing for general preferences." }

  ],

  57: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ walk than take a bus.", opts:["prefer to","would rather","had better","like to"], ans:1, exp:"'Would rather + base verb' = preference." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She'd rather you ____________ (not tell) anyone.", ans:"didn't tell", exp:"'Would rather + subject + past tense' = want someone else not to do it." },

    { type:'error', q:"I would rather to stay home.", ans:"I would rather stay home.", exp:"'Would rather' + base verb (no 'to')." }

  ],

  58: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I'm good _____ remembering faces.", opts:["in","at","for","about"], ans:1, exp:"'Good at' + -ing." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She left without ____________ (say) goodbye.", ans:"saying", exp:"After preposition 'without' → -ing form." },

    { type:'error', q:"I look forward to hear from you.", ans:"I look forward to hearing from you.", exp:"'Look forward to' → 'to' is a preposition here, so use -ing." }

  ],

  59: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ driving on the left. It's normal for me.", opts:["used to","am used to","use to","was used to"], ans:1, exp:"'Be used to + -ing' = accustomed to." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I ____________ (smoke) but I quit.", ans:"used to smoke", exp:"Past habit that stopped → 'used to + base verb'." },

    { type:'error', q:"I am used to wake up early.", ans:"I am used to waking up early.", exp:"'Be used to' + -ing (not to-infinitive)." }

  ],

  60: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I _____ you to help me.", opts:["want","am wanting","wanting","wants"], ans:0, exp:"'Want + object + to-infinitive'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She ____________ (tell) me to be quiet.", ans:"told", exp:"'Tell + object + to-infinitive'." },

    { type:'error', q:"He made me to cry.", ans:"He made me cry.", exp:"'Make + object + base verb' (no 'to')." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ her go. She'll be fine.", opts:["Make","Let","Tell","Allow"], ans:1, exp:"'Let + object + base verb' (no 'to')." }

  ],

  61: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Do you have _____ information?", opts:["an","a","any","some"], ans:2, exp:"'Information' is uncountable → no a/an; use 'any' in questions." },

    { type:'fill', q:"Can I give you ____________ advice?", ans:"some", exp:"Uncountable noun in a positive offer → 'some'." },

    { type:'error', q:"She gave me an advice.", ans:"She gave me some advice / a piece of advice.", exp:"'Advice' is uncountable → no 'an'." }

  ],

  62: [

    { type:'mc', q:"The news _____ very surprising.", opts:["are","were","is","have been"], ans:2, exp:"'News' is always singular in English." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I have a lot of ____________ to do.", ans:"work", exp:"'Work' (tasks) is uncountable." },

    { type:'error', q:"This is a lovely scenery.", ans:"This is lovely scenery.", exp:"'Scenery' is uncountable → no 'a'." }

  ],

  63: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I saw _____ dog in the park. _____ dog was barking.", opts:["a/A","a/The","the/The","the/A"], ans:1, exp:"First mention → 'a'; second mention (already known) → 'the'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She's _____ doctor who treated me.", ans:"the", exp:"Specific doctor (identified by the clause) → 'the'." },

    { type:'error', q:"The life is short.", ans:"Life is short.", exp:"General abstract meaning → no article." }

  ],

  64: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Can you close _____ door?", opts:["a","an","the","—"], ans:2, exp:"'The' when it's clear which one (the door in this room)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"He's _____ best player on the team.", ans:"the", exp:"Superlative → always use 'the'." }

  ],

  65: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ blind need special assistance.", opts:["A","The","—","An"], ans:1, exp:"'The + adjective' = group of people (the blind = blind people)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She swam across _____ English Channel.", ans:"the", exp:"'The' with named bodies of water." },

    { type:'error', q:"I live in the France.", ans:"I live in France.", exp:"No article with most country names." }

  ],

  66: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She went to _____ hospital. (as a patient)", opts:["the","a","—","an"], ans:2, exp:"'Go to hospital' (as patient) → no article in British English." },

    { type:'fill', q:"He goes to _____ school by bus. (he's a student)", ans:"", exp:"'Go to school' (as a student) → no article." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I went to _____ hospital to visit her. (as visitor)", opts:["—","the","a","an"], ans:1, exp:"Visiting as an outsider → 'the hospital' (specific building)." }

  ],

  67: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ dogs are loyal animals.", opts:["The","A","—","Some"], ans:2, exp:"General statement about all dogs → no article." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ crime is increasing. (general statement)", ans:"", exp:"General abstract concept → no article." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ people prefer summer. (most, not the most)", opts:["Most of the","Most","The most","All the"], ans:1, exp:"'Most + noun' for general majority → no 'the'." }

  ],

  68: [

    { type:'error', q:"I need an advice.", ans:"I need some advice.", exp:"'Advice' is uncountable → no 'an'." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She's _____ nurse.", opts:["a","the","an","—"], ans:0, exp:"'A' for professions (one of many nurses)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ sun rises in the east.", ans:"The", exp:"'The' for unique things." }

  ],

  69: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Is there _____ bread left?", opts:["some","any","a","—"], ans:1, exp:"'Any' in questions and negative sentences." },

    { type:'fill', q:"Would you like _____ tea? (offer)", ans:"some", exp:"'Some' in offers (expecting yes)." },

    { type:'error', q:"I don't want some coffee.", ans:"I don't want any coffee.", exp:"Negative sentences → 'any' (not 'some')." }

  ],

  70: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I have _____ money. (zero)", opts:["no","none","not","any"], ans:0, exp:"'No' + noun for zero quantity." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ of the answers were correct.", ans:"None", exp:"'None of' + the/my + noun." },

    { type:'mc', q:"There weren't _____ problems.", opts:["no","none","any","some"], ans:2, exp:"Negative + 'any' = no problems." }

  ],

  71: [

    { type:'mc', q:"There isn't _____ time.", opts:["many","a few","much","a lot"], ans:2, exp:"'Much' + uncountable noun in negatives." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I have _____ friends in London. (some, positive)", ans:"a few", exp:"'A few' = some (positive meaning)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ people know the answer. (hardly any)", opts:["A few","Few","Some","Many"], ans:1, exp:"'Few' (without 'a') = hardly any (negative meaning)." }

  ],

  72: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ children need love. (general)", opts:["All of","All of the","All","Every"], ans:2, exp:"'All + noun' (no 'of') for general statements." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ of my friends are married.", ans:"Most", exp:"'Most of + my/the + noun' for specific group." },

    { type:'error', q:"All of students passed.", ans:"All of the students passed. / All students passed.", exp:"'All of' needs the/my etc. before the noun." }

  ],

  73: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ windows were open.", opts:["Both","Neither","Either","All"], ans:0, exp:"'Both' = the two together (positive)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ answer was correct. (not one, not the other)", ans:"Neither", exp:"'Neither' = not one and not the other (two options)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ day is fine with me. (one or the other)", opts:["Both","Neither","Either","All"], ans:2, exp:"'Either' = one or the other (a choice between two)." }

  ],

  74: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I waited _____ day.", opts:["all","whole","every","each"], ans:0, exp:"'All day' = the whole day (duration)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ student passed the exam.", ans:"Every", exp:"'Every + singular noun' = all students, one by one." },

    { type:'mc', q:"He spent _____ summer travelling.", opts:["all","every","the whole","each"], ans:2, exp:"'The whole + singular noun' = complete, nothing missing." }

  ],

  85: [

    { type:'mc', q:"The lesson was _____. I was _____.", opts:["interesting/interested","interested/interesting","bored/boring","boring/bored"], ans:0, exp:"-ing for the cause; -ed for the person who feels it." },

    { type:'error', q:"I'm very boring at this party.", ans:"I'm very bored at this party.", exp:"People feel bored (-ed); parties/situations are boring (-ing)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"The news was _____. She was _____.", ans:"shocking / shocked", exp:"Shocking = causes shock. Shocked = experiences shock." }

  ],

  87: [

    { type:'mc', q:"He's a _____ driver.", opts:["carefully","careful","care","caring"], ans:1, exp:"Adjective before noun: 'careful driver'." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She speaks English very ____________.", ans:"well", exp:"Adverb modifies verb: 'well' (not 'good')." },

    { type:'error', q:"She speaks English good.", ans:"She speaks English well.", exp:"Adverb needed to modify verb: 'speaks' → 'well'." }

  ],

  88: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She works _____. She's very dedicated. (adverb)", opts:["hardly","hard","hardily","hard work"], ans:1, exp:"'Hard' = adverb of manner (works with effort)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ works. She's very lazy.", opts:["hard","hardly","hard-working","hardily"], ans:1, exp:"'Hardly' = almost not (she barely works)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I haven't seen him ____________. (recently)", ans:"lately", exp:"'Lately' = recently." }

  ],

  89: [

    { type:'mc', q:"It was _____ a cold day that we stayed inside.", opts:["so","such","very","too"], ans:1, exp:"'Such a + noun phrase'." },

    { type:'mc', q:"It was _____ cold that we stayed inside.", opts:["such","so","very","too"], ans:1, exp:"'So + adjective'." },

    { type:'error', q:"It was such cold.", ans:"It was so cold.", exp:"'So' + adjective (no noun). 'Such a' + noun." }

  ],

  90: [

    { type:'mc', q:"This coffee is _____ hot to drink.", opts:["so","such","too","very"], ans:2, exp:"'Too' = more than desired (negative implication)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She wasn't old ____________ to see the film.", ans:"enough", exp:"Adjective + 'enough'." },

    { type:'error', q:"She is enough old.", ans:"She is old enough.", exp:"'Enough' comes AFTER the adjective." }

  ],

  92: [

    { type:'mc', q:"This car is _____ than that one.", opts:["more cheap","cheaper","most cheap","cheapest"], ans:1, exp:"Short adjective → add -er." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She's ____________ (intelligent) than her brother.", ans:"more intelligent", exp:"Long adjective → more + adjective." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I feel _____ today than yesterday.", opts:["more good","gooder","better","more better"], ans:2, exp:"Irregular comparative: good → better." }

  ],

  93: [

    { type:'mc', q:"He's _____ better now.", opts:["very","much","so","more"], ans:1, exp:"'Much/far/a lot' strengthen comparatives." },

    { type:'fill', q:"The _____ I practise, the _____ I become.", ans:"more / more confident", exp:"'The + comparative, the + comparative' structure." }

  ],

  94: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She's _____ tall _____ her brother. (equal)", opts:["so/as","as/as","more/than","too/to"], ans:1, exp:"Equal comparison: as + adjective + as." },

    { type:'fill', q:"It costs ____________ as much as last year.", ans:"twice", exp:"'Twice as much as' = two times the amount." }

  ],

  95: [

    { type:'mc', q:"This is _____ longest river in the world.", opts:["a","—","the","an"], ans:2, exp:"Always 'the' before superlatives." },

    { type:'fill', q:"That was _____ worst meal I've ever had.", ans:"the", exp:"Superlative → always use 'the'." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She's _____ talented student in the class.", opts:["more","the most","most","very most"], ans:1, exp:"'The most + long adjective' = superlative." }

  ],

  96: [

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ it was raining, we went out.", opts:["Despite","In spite of","Although","Because"], ans:2, exp:"'Although' + clause (not noun/-ing)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"_____ the rain, we went out.", ans:"Despite / In spite of", exp:"'Despite/In spite of' + noun." },

    { type:'error', q:"Despite it was cold, we went out.", ans:"Although it was cold, / Despite the cold, we went out.", exp:"'Despite' + noun/-ing (not a clause with subject + verb)." }

  ],

  97: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Take an umbrella _____ it rains.", opts:["if","although","in case","unless"], ans:2, exp:"'In case' = precaution (not the same as 'if')." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I wrote it down _____ I forgot.", ans:"in case", exp:"'In case' = as a precaution." },

    { type:'error', q:"Take an umbrella in case it will rain.", ans:"Take an umbrella in case it rains.", exp:"After 'in case' → present simple (not will)." }

  ],

  98: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I'll come _____ it rains. (= if it doesn't rain)", opts:["if","unless","although","in case"], ans:1, exp:"'Unless' = if not." },

    { type:'fill', q:"You can borrow the car _____ you're careful.", ans:"as long as", exp:"'As long as' = on condition that." }

  ],

  101: [

    { type:'mc', q:"The meeting is _____ Monday.", opts:["at","in","on","by"], ans:2, exp:"'On' + days of the week." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She arrived _____ March.", ans:"in", exp:"'In' + months." },

    { type:'error', q:"She arrived in Monday.", ans:"She arrived on Monday.", exp:"'On' with days: on Monday, on Tuesday." },

    { type:'mc', q:"I'll see you _____ 3 o'clock.", opts:["in","on","at","by"], ans:2, exp:"'At' for precise times." }

  ],

  102: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She's _____ the kitchen.", opts:["at","on","in","by"], ans:2, exp:"'In' for enclosed spaces." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I'll meet you _____ the airport.", ans:"at", exp:"'At' for specific points/locations." },

    { type:'mc', q:"The book is _____ the table.", opts:["in","at","on","by"], ans:2, exp:"'On' for surface contact." }

  ],

  104: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Can you finish it _____ Monday?", opts:["until","by","in","on"], ans:1, exp:"'By' = deadline (no later than)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I usually go to work _____ train.", ans:"by", exp:"'By' + transport." },

    { type:'mc', q:"The play was written _____ Shakespeare.", opts:["by","from","with","of"], ans:0, exp:"'By' for the agent in passive sentences." }

  ],

  118: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I fell asleep _____ the film.", opts:["while","for","during","since"], ans:2, exp:"'During' + noun (the film)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I waited _____ two hours.", ans:"for", exp:"'For' + period of time." },

    { type:'error', q:"I met her during three months.", ans:"I met her for three months.", exp:"'For' + period of time; 'during' + named period." }

  ],

  119: [

    { type:'mc', q:"I'll be ready _____ 6.", opts:["until","by","since","for"], ans:1, exp:"'By' = deadline." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I'll wait _____ 6.", ans:"until", exp:"'Until' = continue to that point." },

    { type:'mc', q:"_____ the time I arrived, she had left.", opts:["By","Until","In","At"], ans:0, exp:"'By the time' + clause = at the point when." }

  ],

  121: [

    { type:'mc', q:"Have you _____ eaten? (sooner than expected)", opts:["still","yet","already","just"], ans:2, exp:"'Already' = sooner than expected (positive)." },

    { type:'fill', q:"I haven't finished ____________.", ans:"yet", exp:"'Yet' at the end of negatives (until now)." },

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ lives at home. (continuing)", opts:["already","yet","still","even"], ans:2, exp:"'Still' = continuing, not stopped." }

  ],

  140: [

    { type:'mc', q:"She _____ me that she was leaving.", opts:["said","told","spoke","talked"], ans:1, exp:"'Tell' needs an object: told me." },

    { type:'fill', q:"She _____ she was leaving.", ans:"said", exp:"'Say' doesn't need an object." },

    { type:'error', q:"He told that he was tired.", ans:"He said he was tired. / He told me he was tired.", exp:"'Tell' always needs a person object." }

  ]

};

// ============================================================

// ALL-TOPIC QUESTION POOL for Exam Mode

// ============================================================

const EXAM_POOL = [

  { unit:1,  q:"Right now, Sarah _____ in the car.",         opts:["drive","drives","is driving","drove"],          ans:2, exp:"Present continuous for NOW." },

  { unit:2,  q:"Water _____ at 100°C.",                     opts:["is boiling","boils","boil","has boiled"],        ans:1, exp:"General truth → present simple." },

  { unit:5,  q:"Mozart _____ in 1756.",                     opts:["is born","was born","has been born","born"],     ans:1, exp:"Specific past event → past simple." },

  { unit:6,  q:"I _____ home when it started to rain.",     opts:["walk","walked","was walking","have walked"],     ans:2, exp:"Background action → past continuous." },
